lg.l: Þe first day of þas fiften days
Þe se sal ryse als þa bukes says
Abowen þe heght of ilka mountayne
Fully fourty cubyttes certayne
And in his stede even upstande
Als an heghe hille dus on þe lande
Þe secunde day þe se sal be swa law
Þat unnethes men sal it knaw
Þe thred day þe se sal seme playn
And stand even in his cours agay[n]
Als it stode ‘first’ at þe bygynnyng
Withouten mare rysyng or fallyng
Þe fierth day sal swilk a wonder be
Þe mast wondreful fisshes of þe se
Sal com togyder and mak swilk romyng
Þat it sal be hydus til mans heryng
Bot what þat romiyng sal signify
Na man may whit bot God almyghty
Þe fift day þe se sal brynne
And alle [þe] watters als þai sal rynne
An þat sal last fra þe son rysyng
Til þe tyme of þe son doun-gangyng
Þe sext day sal spryng a blody dewe
On grisse and tres als it sal shewe
Þe sevend day byggyn’s’ doun sal falle
And grete castels and tours withalle
Þe eght day hard roches and stanes
Sal strik togyder alle attanes
And ilkan of þam sal other doun cast
And ilkan agayn other hortel fast
Swa þat ilka stan on divers wyse
Sal sonder other in thre partyse
Þe neghend day gret erthe-dyn sal be
Generaly in ilka contre
And swa gret erth-dyn als ‘sal’ be þan
Was never hard sythen þe world bygan
Þe tend day þaraftir to neven
Þe erthe sal be made playn and even
For hilles and valeis sal turned be
Intil playn and made even to se
Þe ellevend day men sal come out
Of caves and holes and wend about
Als wode men þat na witt can
And nane sal spek til other þan
[100ra] Þe twelfte day aftir þe sternes alle
And þe signes fra þe heven sal falle
Þe thretend day sal dede men banes
Be sett togyder and ryse al attanes
And aboven on þair graves stand
Þis sal byfalle in ilka land
Þe fourtend day al þat lyves þan
Sal dighe childe man and woman
For þai shalle with þam rys ogayn
Þat byfor war dede outher til ioy or payn
Þe fiftend day þos sal betyde
Alle þe world sal bryn on ilk syde
And þe erthe whar we now duelle
Until þe utter end of alle helle
Þus tels Ierom þer takens fiftene
Als ‘he’ in þe bokes of Ebriens had sene.
Þe fyrste day of Þe xv. days
Þe see schal ryse as Þe boke says
Abouene Þe heyÞe of euere ilke montayne
Ffully fourty cubites for certayne ["ffully" written in left margin]
And euen in hys stede up stonde
As hylles doÞ here in Þe londe
In two days hyt schal be lawe [n.b. "two days"]
Þat unneÞes men schal hyt knowe
Þe iii. day Þe see schal hyt seme playne [“Þe see” inserted above line with a caret]
And stonde euen in hys cours agayne
As hyt stod at Þe bygynnyng
WyÞ outen rysyng or fallyng
Þe iiii. day Þus schal hyt be
Þe moste wondyrful part Þe fysches of Þe see [“part” abbreviated; “p” with stroke on descender, “at”]
Schal come to gedyr & make romyyng [erasure in “romyng,” the first “y”]
Fful hedus unto mannes heryng
But wat hyt schal sygnefy
Wote no man but god only
Þe v. day Þe see schal brynne
And waters al Þat buÞ Þer inne
And Þat schal laste fro Þe sonne rysyng
Unto Þe tyme of doune goyng
Þe vi. day schal spryng blody dew
On gres on tres as Þen schal schew
Þe vii. day schal doune byggynges falle [“s” in “byggynges” superscripted]
And gret castelles & toures wyÞ alle
Þe viii day roches and stones [49r.]
Schal smyte togedere alle at ones
Eche on at oÞere schal smyte faste
So Þat eche one schal oÞere doune caste
And eche ston in dyuers wyse
Schal be on sonderte of Þre partyse
Þe ix. day schal erthequake be
Generaly in eche contre
So gret erthequave [sic] as schal be Þan
Was neuere sen seyÞe Þe world by gan [“seyÞe” superscripted with caret]
Þe x. day affter to nemen
Þe erthe schal be made playn and euen [“wyÞ wynd” written above “and euen”]
Hylles and dales schal turnede be
And be made alle euen to se
Þe xi. day schal come ougte
Of cauys and holys abougte
As wode men Þat no wygt can
And non schal speke to oÞer Þan
Þe xii. day affter Þe sterres alle
And Þe syngnys schal fro heuene falle
Þe xiii. day schul [sic] dede men bones
Be set togedere and ryse at ones
And up on here graues stonde
Þys schal befalle in eche londe
Þe xiiii. day al Þat leueÞ Þan
Schal dye chyld man & wommman
Ffor Þey wyÞ hem schul ryse agayne
Þat dide byfore to ioye or peyne
Þe xv. day schal Þus bytyde
Þe world schal brenne on eche a syde
Þat Þe erthe Þere we now dwelle
Unto Þe utteriest party of helle
Þus telleÞ Ierome Þys tokenes xv.
As he in hebrew bokes hadde sene.
Þe first day of Þe xv dayes
Þe see schal a ryse as Þe boke sayes
Abouyn Þe heygte of euery mountayne
Fourti cubytes for certayne
And euene in his stede upryght stande
As here don trees in Þis lande [71v; blue and red paraphs for each sign start]
The secunde day it schal be so lawe [flourished ascender on the initial T]
Þat unneÞe men schul it knawe
Þe Þridde day it schal semyn pleyn
And stonde euene in his cours ageyn [three line curved flourish in left margin out from A]
As it stode at Þe bygynnyng
Withowten risyng or fallyng
Þe ferÞe day Þus schall it be
Þe moste wonderful sygt of Þe see [n.b. "sygt" definitely s, not f]
Þat schal come togider & make roryng
Fful hydous unto mannys heryng
But what it schal signefye
Þer [sic; abbreviated] wote no man but god on lyue [sic]
Þe fyfte day Þe see schal brenne
And wateris all Þat aren Þerinne
And Þat schal laste fro Þe sonne rysyng
Unto Þe tyme of Þe sonne doun goyng
Þe sixt day Þer schal sprynge a blody dewe
On gras on trees as Þey schul [sic] schewe
Þe seueneÞ day byggynges schole [sic] doun falle
And grete castels and toures wiÞ alle
Þe viii day roches and stonys
Schole strike togyderis alle atonys
Euery to oÞer schal stryken faste
So Þat alle Þey schole doun caste
And euery stone on sundri wise
ScholeÞ ben sundryd on sundry partise
Þe ix day schal Þe erÞe dene be
Generally in euery cuntre
So grete erÞe dene as schal ben Þan
Was Þer neuer siÞ Þe world bygan
Þe x day after to neuene
Þe erÞe schal be made playn & euene [72r]
Hylles and valeys schul turnyd be
And be made all euene forto se
Þe xi day Þer schal come oute
Of cafes & hilles al abowte
As dede men Þat no wit ne kan
And none schal speke to oÞer Þan
Þe xii day after Þe sterris alle
And Þe signe for heuene scholen doun falle
Þe xiii day schole mennis bonys
Ben set togyderis and risin [sic] alle atonys
And upon here grauis stande
Þat schal byfalle in euery lande
The xiiii day all Þat leuiÞ Þan
Schal dye boÞe childe man & womman
Ffor Þey wiÞ hem schal risen ageyn
Þat dyeden byfore to ioye or to peyn
Þe xv day it schal Þus bytyde
Þe world schal brenne on euery side [sic]
And Þe erÞe Þere we now inne dwelle
Unto Þe utterest part of helle
Þus telliÞ Ierome Þese tokenys fiftene
As he in Ebrew bokys hadde hem sene
The furste day of þe xv dayes [T, two line red initial]
Þe see schel aryse þe bok primus saies ["primus" abbreviated]
Abowe þe eyest montayne
Ffourty cubites al certayne
And euene stille in stede stonde
As doþ here hulles in þe londe
þe secunde day hit schel be lowe
þat uneþe men schulle hit knowe
þe þerde day hit schel seme playne
and stonde in his cors agayne
as hit stod ate bygynnyg [sic]
withoute rysing oþer fallyng
þe ferþe day 3it schel hit bue [sic]
most wonder þat fy3es of þe se
schul come togedre & make gronyng
Fful deulful unto mannis heryng
bote wat hit schel signifye
wot noman bote god onlye
Þ day pe see schel brenne
And al þe water þat is þerynne
And þat schel leste fro sunne rysyng
Unto þe tyme of doun goyng
Þe sixte day schel sprynge blod dewe
In gras in trees as hy wolle schewe
þe soveþe day hous schulle doun falle
And grete castell & toures alle
Þe vii day roches & stonys
Schulle sym [struck through] smyte togedres al at onys
Echone oþer schel smyte faste
So þat alle schel be doun caste [55v]
And euereche stone in diuerse wyse
Schel be departyd in diuerse partyse
þe nyþe day schel erþe quake bue [sic]
Generaliche in eche contre
So gret 3ourþ [sic?] quakyng as schel be þanne
Was never siþþe þe worlde byganne
þe teþe day after to mene
þe erþe schel bue playn & euene
Hulles & waleis schulleþ tornid be
& be mad al euene to þe see
Pe xi day men schollep comen oute
Of caues & of fondes [?] aboute
As wode men þat no wyt can
& non schel speke to oþer þan
þe xii day þe sterres alle
And signes fro heuene adoun schel falle
þe xiii day schulleþ dede bones
Be set togadre & arise at onys
And oppon þe berell stonde
þis schel befallen in eche londe
þe furtepe day hy þat lyneþ þanne
schulleþ daye [sic] child womman & manne
ffor hy wiþ ham schulleþ ryse a3eyne
þat dayde byfore to ioye oþer payne
þe xv day schel is bytyde
þe worlde schel brenne in eche syde
and al erþe her þer we duelle
unto þe dupe pyt of helle
þis telleþ synt Ierom toknes xv
þat he in boke of ebrou hadde sene
Þe first day of Þe xv dayes
Þe see sal ryse as Þe boke sais
Abouen Þe heght of ilk mountayn
Ffully fourty cubites Þis is certayne
And in his stede up to stande
As a high hill dose on Þe lande
Þe secunde day sal Þe see be so logh
Þat unnethes men sal it knawe
Þe iii day sal Þe see seme playne
And stand euen in his coures [sic] agayne
Als it stode first at Þe begynnyng
With outen more rysyng or fallynge
Þe forth day sal suilke wonder be
Þe most wounder fysshe on Þe see
Sal come sanme [sic] and make slike romyng
Þat it sal be hydous to mans herynge [59v]
Bot what Þat romyng sal signifye
No man wote bot god almygty
Þe fifte day Þe see sal brenne
And alle Þe watres as Þai sal renne
And sal laste fro Þe sunne rysynge [sic, no "that"]
Unto Þe tyme of Þe sunne doune fallynge
Þe vi day sal springe a blody dewe
On gresse and trees als it sal schewe
Þe seuent biggens sal doune falle
And grete castels and toures withalle
Þe aght day herde roches and stones
Sal stryke togedir alle at ones
And ilkon sal oÞer doune caste
And ilkon agayne other hurle faste
So Þat ilk stone on diuerse wise
Sal sundir other in thre partys
Þe nygt day grete erthenes sal be [ertheues?]
Generally in ilke cuntre
And so gret erthenes as sal be Þan
Was neuer herde sen Þe worlde began
Þe tent day Þerafter to neuen
Þe erthe sal be made playne and euen
Ffor hilles and vailes alle turned sal be
To playn : and made euen to se
Þe elleuent day men sal come oute
Of cayues and hoyles and wende aboute
As wode men Þat no wit kan
And none sal speke til oÞer Þan
Þe twelfte day after Þe sternes alle
And Þe signes fro heuen sal falle
Þe thryttynde day sal ded men bones
Be sett to gedir and ryse at ones
And obouen on Þaire graues stande
Þis sal befalle in ilka lande
Þe xiiii day alle Þat lyues Þan
Sal dye childe : woman and man [60r]
Ffor Þai with Þam bus ryse agayne ["bus" is clear]
Þat first were dede to ioye or payne
Þe fyftenede day Þus sal betide
Alle Þe worlde sal brenne on ilka syde
And Þe erthe wer we now duelle
Unto Þe uiter erthe of alle helle [u in uiter clear. Followed by a small minim]
Þus telles Ierome Þes tokenes xv [I in Ierome touched in red]
As he in Þe bokes of Ebryeus has sene. [E touched in red]
Þe ferste day þe se xal ryson : & bouen þe heyheste mountayne
Ffourty kubites on heythe as toures : & torettes stonden for certayne
Þe secunde day it xal falle yendon ful lowe
Þat ful besilyche ony man xal it kennen or knowe
Þe thredde day it xal be playn as it was at þe gynenge
With owte ebbs or flowenge : rysynge or fallenge
[day four missing; following couþlet misarranged and text missing; pencil couplet bracket does extend into lower margin]
Þefyfte day þe se xal brenne : from þe sunne rysenge to son [66r]
And alle þe watres þerenne : til þat þe sunne goth doun agen
Þe sexte day sal sprenge a ful gresely blody dewe
Hon gres & on tren : yt schal ful wonderliche schewe
Seuene day castelles & toures : & sytes xul falle
And townes turetes & tren also with alle
Þe eygthe day roches & stones xul feyten on dyuerse wise
And yche of hem xal other breke on sundry partise
Þe Neygthe day xal herthedene be in eueryche lond & cuntre þan
Sweche othere meruayles weren neuer sen þe werd bygan
Þe tende day xal al herthe be made playne euene to be
Helles & valeyhes schul by alle lyche on to se
Eleuene day xul alle dede men comen hout of her graues & kragges þan
And non of hem xal speke no word to other þan
Þe twelue day after : þe sterres : bothe grete & smale alle
And also othere sengnes from þe heuene xul doun falle
Þe xiiitene day xul alle dede menes bones gon togeder & up stande
And rysen alle at ones : thus xal it ben in yche lande
Þe xiiiiene day alle þo þat lyuen þanne : xul deygen for certayne
And rysen agen with alle othere þat han ben in ioye or payne
Þe xv day al þe werd xal brenne : þer we hen dwelle
And also al herthe into þe nethereste peth of helle
Thus telleth ierom þat alle thes tokenes xul be
But yet may no man : weten for certayn for it is godes þriute
Þe first day of Þese : xv dayis
Þe see schalle ryse : saynt ierome sayis
Abouyn Þe heyht : of uche a montayne
Ffully xl cubitis certayne [no colon]
And in his stede : euen stande
As an hey walle : doth in lande
Þe secounde day : hit schal be so lowe
Þat unneth men : schal Þe see knowe
Þe thirde day : god schal so ordayne [no multi-line thorn]
Þat Þe see : schal stonde euyn agayne
As hit was first : at Þe bygynnyng
With outyn more rysyng : or fallyng
Þe ferth day : schal suche a wondyr be
Þe moste wondyrful bestys on to se [no colon]
Schal come togyddr : an [sic] make suche a roryng
Þat hit schal be : grysyly to mannis hyring [sic]
Bot what Þat roryng : schal signify
No man wote : bot gode almyhty
Þe fyfte day : Þe see schal brenne
Ande Þe watris : as Þay schal renne
Ande Þat schal laste : fro Þe sonne rysyng
To Þe tyme of Þe sonne : to Þe rest goyng
Þe sixte day : schal spryng a blody dewe
On gras on treys : hit schal ham schewe
Þe vii day schal grete byldyngys doun falle [no colon]
Ande grete castelys : an tounnys wyth alle [n.b. "towns"]
Þe viii day : harde rochys an stonys [no multi-line thorn]
Schal smyte togydr : alle at onys
Ande uche of ham : schal oÞir doun cast
Ande uche ageyne oÞir : hortyl fast [97, a recto]
So Þat uche a stone : on diuers wyse
Schal brekyn : on thre parties
Þe ix day : Þe erthe quake schal be
And so outrage : in uche a contre [n.b. "outrage"]
Ande so moche : as schal be Þan
Was neuir : sethyn Þe worlde bygan
Þe x day : aftirward to neuen
Þe erth schal be makyde : playne an euen
Ffor hyllys an valleyis : schal turnyd be
Al to playne : an euyn contre
Þe xi day : men schal come oute
Of hillys an cauys : an wandyr aboute
And as wode men : Þat no gode can
And none schal spek : to oÞir Þan
Þe xii day : Þe sterris alle
Ande Þe signis : fro heuyn schal falle
Þe xiii : day schal dede men bonys [no thorn multi-line initial]
Be sette togydderys : an ryse at onys
And aboue har grauis : riht up stande
Ffor Þus schal falle : in uche a lande
Þe xiiii day : al Þat lyuythe Þan
Schal deye : man childe an womman
Ffor Þay schal ryse : with ham agayne
Þat byfor : were dede to ioye or payne
Þe xv day : Þus schal betide
Þe worlde schal brenne : on uche a syde
And Þe erthe : whar we dwelle
To Þe ferthest ende : Þat is of helle
Þus tellyth Ierome : Þese tokenys fiftene
As he in ebru boke : hade ham sene
Þe first day of þas xv days
Þe se sall rise as seint Ierome says
Abouen þe heght of ilka mountayne
Ffully fourty cubites as for certayne
And in his stede euen up stand
Als an heghe wall dos on þe land
Þe secunday [sic] it sall be so lawe
Þat unnethes sall men þe se knaw
Þe iii day god sall so ordeyne
Þat þe se sall stand euen agayne
Als it was first at þe begynnyng
Withe outen more risyng or fallyng
Þe iiii day sall swilk a wonder be
Þe most wonderful bestes in þe se
Sall come togedir & make swilk romyng
Þat it sall be hidous to mans heryng
Bot what þat romyng sall signify
No man wote bot god all mighty
Þe v day þe se sall bryn
And þe waters als þai sall ryn [61r]
And þat sall last fro þe son rysyng
Till þe tyme of þe son don gangyng
Þe vi day sall spryng a blody dew
On gresse & tres sall it schew
Þe vii day bigyns sall don fall
An grete castels & toures withe all
Þe viii day hard roches & stanes
Sall strike to gedir all at anes
And ilkone of þam sall oþer doun cast
And ogayn oþer hurall [sic] fast
So þat ilka stane on diuers wyse
Sall sonder oþer on diuers partyse ["wyse" crossed out before "partyse"]
Þe ix day erthedyn sall be
So verally in alka cuntre [sic for line]
And so mekyll als sall be þan
Was neuer here sen þe werld began
Þe x day þare efter to neuen
Þe erthe sall be made playne & euen
Ffor hilles & valays sall turned be
All in to playne & euen to se
Þe xi day men sall com oute
Of holes & caues & wende aboute
Als wode men þat no wit can
And none sall spek to oþer þan
Þe xii day after þe sternes all
And þe signes fro þe heuen sall fall
Þe xiii day sall deade men banes
Be sett togedir & rise at anes
And abouen upon þaire graues stand
Ffor þus sall fall in ilka land
Þe xiiii day all þat lyues þan
Sall dye man child & woman
Ffor þai sall withe þaim rise ogayne
Þat before wer dede to ioy or payne [61v]
Þe xv day þus sall betide [ascenders for Þ, Þ, l, l, b, d]
Þe werld sall bryn on ilk a syde
And þe erthe whare we now dwell
Un till þe utter end of all hell
Þus telles Ierome þas taknes fiftene
Als he in heubreus [sic] bukes haues sene
The firste day of þe fiftene dayes
Þe see shalle rise as þe boke sayes
Aboue þe heygte of eche mounteyne
Ffuly fourty cubites certeyne
And in his stede up stonde
As an hye hille doþ on þe londe
Þe seconde day þe see shal be lowe
Þat unneþe men shulle him knowe
Þe þridde day þe see shalle seeme playne
And stonde euene in his cowrs ageyne
As hit stode at þe firste bigynnynge
Withouten more risyng or fallynge
Þe ferþe day shalle suche a wonder be
Þat þe moste wondrefull fisshe of þe see
Shal come togider & make suche a romynge
Þat hit shal be hidouse to mannys herynge
But what þat romynge shal signifie
No man may wite but god almygty
Þe fifte day þe see shalle brenne
And alle þe watres as þey shulle renne
And þat shal laste from þe sonne rysynge
To þe laste of þe sonne doune goynge
Þe sixte day shall springe a blody dewe
On grasse & tres þat hit shal shewe
Þe seuenþe day þe bildyng shal doun falle
And þe grete castelles & towres withalle
Þe eygte day harde roches & stones
Schulle strike tigoder alle atones
And eche of hem shal oþer doun caste
And eche ageyne oþer shalle rotil faste
So þat eche stone schalle breke oþer on eche partise
Þe nyþe day grete erþe dynne shal be
Generalliche in eche countre
And so grete erþe dynne shal be þan
Was neuer herde siþen þe world bigan
Þe teþe day after to neuene
Hillis & valeys shulle be turned euene
In to playne & made euene to see
In alle manere countre
Þe elleuenþe day men shulle some oute
Of caues & hooles & wende aboute
As wode men þat no wyt can
And noone shal speke to oþer þan

[27r] The fyrst day of Þe fyftene days / the se shall ryse as the boke says
Abouyn the heyght of euery mowntayn / fowrty cubytys in certain
And euyn in hys stede upstand / as hyllys doth here in thys land
The second day yt shal be law / Þat onnethys ony man shal yt knaw
The thryd day yt shal seme playn / and stond euyn in hys cours ageyn
As yt stood at the begynnyng / without rysyng or fallyng
The fowrthe day thus shal yt be / the most wondyr fysshys of the se
Shal com togedyr and make remenyng / full hydous to mans heryng
But qwat [sic] Þat yt shal sygnyfy / wote noman but god almyghty
The fyfte day the see shal bryn / and the watyrs as they ryn
And that shal last fro the sunne rysyng / untyl the tyme of the down goyng
The syxte day shal spryng a blody dew / on gres and treis as it shal shew
The seuent day howsyng shal doun fall / & grete castellys and towrs with all [n.b. “howsyng”]
The eyghte day rochys & stonys / shal smyght togedyr all at onys
Echeon to othyr shal smyght fast / so Þat they shal be all down cast
And euery stone on dyuers wys / shal be sundryd in thre partys
The neynt day shal ertheqwavys be / generally in euery cuntre
So grete ertheqwavys as shal be than / was neuyr sethyn the world began
The tenthe day aftyr to nevyn / the erthe shal be made pleyn & euyn
Hellys [sic] and valys shal turenyd be / and be made all euyn to se
The elleuenthe day men shal com out / of cauys & holys all abowt
As wode men that no wyt can / and none shal speke to othyr than
The twelthe day aftyr the sterrys shal fall / & sygnys fro the heuyn all
The thrydtenthe day shal dede men bonys / be sett togedyr & ryse at onys
And upon ther gravys stande / thys shal befall in euery lande
The fowrtenthe day all that leuyth Þan / shal dye chyld man & woman
Ffor they wyth them shal ryse ageyn / Þat dyed byfor to ioye or payn
The fyftenthe day shal thus betyde / the world shal bren on euery syde
On the erthe where we now duelle / unto the utteryst ende of helle
Thus tellyth Ierom thes toknys fyftene / as he in Ebrew bokys had sene.

Gedre an rise at ones
And opon Þaire graues ston
De Þis shal befalle in iche
Londe Þe fouretent dai
Þat liuen Þen shal dee
Shild [sic] mon & woman ffor
Þai shal with hem rise again
Þat before were ded to ioie
Or Þain Þe fiftent dai
Þus shal betide Þe world
Shal brenne on iche side
And Þe erthe Þere we
Dwelle unto Þe utterest
Ende of helle Þus telles
Ierome Þese tokenes fifte
Ne as in bokes of hebre
Ues he had sene.
Þe fyrste day of þes fyftene days [135v]
Þe see schal ryse seynt ierom says
Aboue þe heght of echa mountayne
Ffully fourty cubytus certeyne
And in his stede euene stonde
As an hye val doþ on the londe [note: a high valley]
Þe secunde day it schal be so lowe
Þat unneþe schal men þe se knowe
[136r] Þe thrid day god wyl so ordayne
Þe see schal stonde euene agayne
As it was at þe fyrste bygynnyng
Wit oute more rysing or fallyng
Þe ferþe day schal soche a wondur be
Þe moste wondurful bestes on to se [generic beasts, not sea creatures]
Schal come togedre & make soche a roryng
Þat it schal be grysly to mans heryng [grisly, not hideous]
Bot wat þat roryng schal sygnyfy
No man wote bot god only
Þe v day þe see schal brenne
And þe wateres as þai renne
And þat schal laste fro þe sonne rysyng
Til þe sonne go to restyng [resting, not down going]
Þe sexte day schal spring a blody dewe
On gres and on trees it schal hym schewe
Þe vii day schal grete byldyngs doune falle
And grete castells and tounes wyt alle [towns, not towers]
Þe viii day harde roches and stones
Schal smyte to gedre alle attones
And echon schal oþur doune caste
And echone hurtul wyt oþur faste
So þat echa stone in dyuers wyse
Schal departe in thre þartyse
Þe ix day þe erþe quake schal be
So outerage in euery cuntre [outerage]
And so myche as schal be þan
Wan [sic] neuer sethe þe worlde bygan
Þe x day þer aftur to neuene
Þe erthe schal be made pleyne & euene
For hilles and valeys schul torned be
Alle to euene and pleyne contre
Þe xi day men schal come oute
Of hilles and caues and wandur aboute
And as wode men þat no gode can
[136v] And none schal sþeke tul oþur þan
Þe xii day þe sterres alle
And þe sygnes fro heuen schal falle
Þe xiii day schal dede mens bones
Be sette togedur and ryse alle atones
And abouen her graues stonde
And þis schal falle in echa londe
Þe xiiii day all þat lyuen þan
Schul dye man childe and woman
Ffor þai schal ryse with hem agayne
Þat before were dede to ioye or payne
Þe xv day þis schal bytyde
Þe worlde schal brenne on euery syde
And þe erthe þer we dwelle
To þe ferrest ende of helle
Þus telleþ seynt ierom þes toknes fiftene
As he in ebrewe boke had hem sene
Þe furste day of þe fiftene days [five line initial]
Þe see schal rise þe bok says
Aboue þe hei3þe of all mountayne

And euene in his stude stonde

Þe secounde day schal beo so lowe
Þat unneþe men schul hit knowe [second e in unneþe superscripted]
Þe thridde day hit schal seme þleyn
And stonde euene in his cours a3eyn
As hit stod atte biginnyng
Wiþoute rysyng or fallyng
Þe ffeorþe day þus schal hit be

Schal come togedere and make roryng
Wonder hidous to monnes heryng
But what hit schal signefy
Wot non but god almi3ty
Þe ffifþe day þe se schal brenne

Þe sixte dai [sic] schal springe blod [sic] dewe
On gras on treos fulli to schewe
Þe seuenþe day buildinges schal doun falle

Schul smite togedere all at ones
Uche to oþer schal smyte faste
So þat uchone schal doun be caste

Þe niþe day schal eorþe dune be

So gret eorþe dune as schal be þan

Þe tenþe day for to neuene
Eorþe schal be mad pleyn and euene

Þe elleueþe day men schul come oute

Þe twelfþe day þe sterres alle

& euene in his stede up stonde
as hil her doth on þe londe
þe secunde day it shal be lowe
þat un nethis men shal it knowe
The thrydde day shal it seme pleyne
& stondyn euene in his cours ageyne
as it stod atte bygynnyng
With outyn rysyng or fallyng
þe ferthe day þus shal it be
þe most wondyrful fyschis of þe se
Shul come to gedir & make rounyng
fful hidous un to manys heryng
But what it shal signify
Wot no man but god onely
þe fyfthe day þe see shal bren
& þe water al þer in
& leste fro þe sunne rysyng
In to þe tyme of þe doun goyng
þe sexthe day shal spryng blody dewe
On gres on trees as þey shal schewe
þe seuenþe day housyng shal doun falle
& grete castelys & tourys with alle
þe eythþe day roches & stones
Shul smyte to geder al at ones
ich on oþer shal smyte faste
So þat ich on shul doun be cast
ich a ston on sundry wyse
shul ben sundryd in thre partise
The nynthe day shal erþe dene be
Generaly in ich a cuntre
So gret erthe dene as shal be þan
Was neuere sythe þe werld be gan
þe tenthe day after us to neuene
þe erthe shal be mad pleyn & euene
hillys & valys shul turned be
& be mad al euene to se
The elleuenthe day men shal come out
of cauys & of holys about
as wode men þat no good can
& non shal speke to oþer þan
þe twelfthe day after þe sterris alle
& þe signes fro heuene shal falle
The threttenthe day shul dede menys bonys
Ben set to geder & rysyn at onys
& up on her grauis stonde
þis shal be fallyn in ich a londe
The fourtenthe day al þat leue þan
Shul deye man & womman
ffor þey with hem shul rise ageyn
þat deyed by forn to ioye or peyn
The fyftenthe day shal þer be tyde
þe werld shal brennyn on ich a syde
& þe erthe þat we now on dwelle
in to þe uttreste ende of helle
þus tellyth Ierom þese toknes XV
as he in a bok of ebrew hadde sene
þe feyrst day of þese xv days
þe see sal ryse als seynt seys
aboue þe heythe of ilke a mounteyn
ff fully fowrty cubyts al is for serteyn
& in hys stede euene up stande
als an hey wal dos on þe lande
þe secunde day it sal be so lawe
þat unnethes men sal þe see knawe
þe threde day god sal so ordayne
þat þe see sal stande euene agayne
als it was ferst at þe begynnyng
wyth owten more rysyng or fallyng
þe ferþe day sal sulk a wonder be
þe maste wonderful bests in þe see
Shal come to geder & make sulk rouryng
þat it sal be hydus to mannys heryng
but wat þat cryeng sal synefy
no man wot but god al myhty
þe v day þe see sal brynne
& þe waterys als þey sal rynne
& þat sal laste fro þe sonne rysyng
tyl þe tyme of þe sonne down goyng
þe vi day shal sprynge a blody dew
On gres on tres þat sal it schew
þe vii day byggynges sal down falle
& grete castell & tooris wyth alle
þe viii day hard rochys & stones
Shal smyte to gedere alle at ones
& ech of hem down oþer sal caste
& ageyn oþer hute[sic]l faste
So þat ilk stan on dyuers wyse
Sal sonder oþer on þre partyse
þe ix day erthe den sal be
So verayly i ilk a contre
& so mykyl als sal be þan
Was neuere i herd sen þe world began
þe x day þar efter to neuen
þe erthe sal be mad playn & euen
ffor hyll & valays sal turned be
alle in to playn & euene to se
þe xi day men sal come owth
of holes & caues & wende abowth
als wode men þat no wyt can
& non sal spicke to oþer þan
þe xii day after þe sterrys alle
þe syngnes fro þe heuen sal falle
þe xiii daye sal dede mens bones
be set to geder & ryse at ones
& aboue opyn here graues stande
ffor þus sal falle in ilk a lande
þe xiiii day alle þat leues þan
sal deye man chyld & wommaan [sic]
ffor þey sal wyth hem ryse ageyn
þat be for war dede to ioye & peyn
þe xv þus shal be tyde
þe world sal brynne on ilk a syde
in þe erth wer we now dwelle
unto þe utter ende of al helle
þus tell iroeme tokenes xv
als he in hebrus bokes has sene.
[77r] The ferst day of the xv dayes
The se shal ryse as the book seyes
Aboue the heyght of euery montayne
Fful xl cubytys serteyn
And in that stede euer opward
As a hy hyl doth up the land [rhyme fails]
The ii day the se shal be so lowe
That onnethis men shal it knowe
The iii day the se shal seme pleyn
And stonde euyn in his cours ayeyn
As it stood at the ferst bygynnyng
With oute more rysyng or fallyng
The iiiie day soche a wundre shal be [e superscripted, so done where e appears below]
A greet wondryng [sic] of waterys and of pe se
Shal go togederys & make soche a roryng
That it shal be hydous to mannys heryng
But what that roryng shal sygnyfy
No man woot but god almyghty
The ve day the se shal brenne
And al the waterys that shal renne
And that shal last fro the sonne rysyng
Unto the tyme of the doun goyng
The vi day shal aryse a blody dew
On gres and tree as it shal shewe
The vii day housys shal doun falle
And greet castell and tourys alle
The viii day hard rochis and stonys
Shal stryke togedere al at onus [sic]
And euerych ayen othur shal russhe faste [n.b. rush]
[78r] And euerych shal othur doun caste
And al to dryue to asshis small [nb ashes]
And thus shal stonys endyn al
The ixe day greet erthkwaue shal bee
Generally in euery contre
So greet as shal be than
Was neuer sythen erth bygan
The xe day there aftyr to neuen
The erth shal be pleyn and euene
Ffor hill and valeyes shal turnyd bee
In to playn and euene to see
The xie day men shal come out
Of cauys and holus and wandre about
As wode men that noght kan
And noon shal speke wyth othur than
The xiie day aftyr the sterrys alle
And othur sygnys from helle [sic] shal falle [nb hell]
The xiiie day shal dede mennys bonys
Be set togederys al at onus [sic]
And aboue on the graue stande
This shal be seen in euery lande
The xiiiie day al that lyuyth than
Shal deye child man & womman
Fforto aryse wyth theym [sic] ayeyn
That ben ordeynd to ioye or peyn
The xve day thus shal betyde
The world shal brenne on euery syde
And the erth that we in dwelle
[78v] To the ottemast ende of al helle
Thus tellyth Ierom of the tookenys [sic] xve
As in the ebreus book he had hem sene.
The first day of þese xv dayis
þe se schal rese seynt Ierome says
aboue þe heyght of vche montayne
ffully xl cubitus certayne
and in his styde euyn stande
as an hey wal dos on lande
The secounde day hit schal be so lowe
þat vnnethe men schal þe se knowe
The þryd day god schal go ordayne
þat þe se schal stande euyn agayne
As hit was fyrst at þe begynnyng
Withoutyn more rysyng or fallyng
The iiii day schal soche a wondir be
þe most wondirful bestis vnto
Schal come to gedire & make soche a roryng
þat hit schal be grysly to manys hyryng
Bot what þat roryng schal signyfy
No man wote bot god al myghty
The v dat þe se schal brenne
And þe wateris as þay schal renne
And þat schal lest fro þe sone rysyng
Til þe tyme of þe sone to rest goyng
The vi day schal spryng a blody dewe
On grasse on treis hit schal ham schew
The vii day schal grete bildyngis don fall
And grete castelis & townys [sic] with alle
The viii day harde rochis & stonys
Schal smyte to gedire al at onys
And vche of ham schal oþer doun cast
And vche agayn oþer hurtil fast
So þat vche a stone on diuerse wyse
Schal sundryn on þre partise
The ix day þe erth quakyng schal be
And so ont ce [ge?] in vche contre
And so moche qakyng as schal be þan
Was neuer sethyn þe worlde be gan
The x day aftirwarde to neuyne
þe erth schal be made playne & euyn
ffor hillis & valeys schal turn [illegible] be
al to playne & euyne contre
The xi day men schal come oute
of hillis & cauys & Wandir aboute
And as wode men þt no gode can
And none schal speke to oþer þan
The xii day þe sterris alle
And þe signys fro heuyn schal falle
The xiii day schal dede men bonys
Be set to gedire & rise at onys
And aboue harre [sic] grauys stande
ffor þus schal falle in vche lande
The xiiii day al þat lyuyn þan
Schal dey man childe & woman
ffor þay schal ryse with ham agayne
þat befor were dede to ioye or payne
The xv day þus schal betyde
þe worlde schal brene on vche syde
And þe erth þer we dwelle
To þe ferthist ende of helle
þus tellis Ierome þes tokenys fiftene
As he in ebru boke had ham sene
Þe firste of þese xv dayes
þe se schal rise as þe boke says
aboue þe heygest of eny mounteyne
ffully fourty cubites certeyne
and in his stede euen upstonde
as an heig yle doþ in þe lond
þe ii day þe see schal be lowe
þat vnneþe eny man schal hit knowe
þe iii day þe see schal be playne
and stonde euen in his cours ageyne
as it stode firste atte bigynnynge
Wiþout more risinge oþer fallinge
þe iiii day schal sich a wonder be
þat þe moste wonderful fishes of þe see
þat schal be hidouse to mannes herynge
But what þat rorynge schal signifye
Noman wote but god almygty
þe v day þe see schal brene
and alle þe watres þat schal renne
and þat schal laste from sunne risinge
Til þe tyme of sunne doun goynge
þe vi day schal springe a blody dewe
On gras on trees as hit schal schewe
þe vii day bigynnynge doun schal schewe
Grete castelles & tours withalle
þe viii day grete roches & stones
Schal figte togidre al at ones
and echone of hem schal oþer doun caste
and echone agens oþer schal hurtle faste
so þat ech stone in diuerse wise
Schal to berste oþer in diuerse partyes
þe ix day grete erþe ben [sic] schal be
Oueral in ech lond & eche cuntre
and so grete as schal be þan
in eche cuntre herde neuer man
þe x day after to neuene
Schal come a wynde doun from heuen
þat yles & valeyes turned schal be
In to playne & made euen to se
þe xi day men schul come out
of Kaves & holes & wende about
as wode men þat no witt he cem [sic]
and noon schal speke to oþer þem
þe xii day after þe sterres alle
and þe sunne from heuen schal falle
þe xiii day schal dede men bones
Be sette togidre and rise at ones
and on her graves þan stonde
þus schal it falle in ech alonde
þe xiiii day alle þat lyveþ þan
Schal deye childe man & womman
ffor þen wiþ hem schal rise ageyn
þat bifore were to ioye or peyne
þe xv day it schal bityde
þe worlde schal brenne on eche aside
and þe erþe wheron we dwelle
into þe vtterest ende of helle
þus telleþ Ierome þese tokenes fiftene
as he in þe bokes of hebreus had sene.
The furst tokne off þese xv dais
þe see schal rise as Ierome sais
þe heigte aboue eny montayne
þe heig off xl cubites certayne
a [sic] in his stede up stand
as a wal doþ op [sic] þe land
þe secound day hit schal be so lawe
þe unneþes men ne schul þe se knawe
þe þridde dai god schal so ordenie
þat þe see schal stand agenie
as hit wes furst at þe biginning
Wiþ out eny more reising or falling
The furþe dai in þe see schal bee
The most wondurful bestis þat men may see
Schuln come togedur & make such a rowning [sic]
þat hit schal be hidous to mans hering
But what þat rowning schal signefie
No man not but god almygty
þe fifþe day þe see schal brenne
and þe watres þat doþ renne
and þat schal last fro þe sonne rising
To þe time off þe adoun going
The sixte day schal spring a blodi dewe
as gras and trees schuln hit schewe
þe seuenþe dai bigging schul doun falle
Grete castles and touns wiþ alle
þe viii daie hard roches and stones
Schuln smyte to gedre al attones
and uch oþur adoun cast
& uche hurtle agein oþur fast
So þat uche ston on diuers wise
Schal sondre oþur in diuers partie [sic]
The ix day erþe dene schal bee
So verrei in uche contre
and so hidous as hit schal be þanne
Was neuer non seþ þe world biganne
þe x day þer afftur to nempne
þe erþe schal be mad plein and euene
for hulles and valeies schulln icund bee
al plain and euen appon to see
þe xi dai men schuln come out
off holes and caues & wend about
as wod men þat no wit [?]e can
and non schal speke to oþur þan
þe xii day afftur þe sterres alle
and þe signes fram heuen schuln falle
þe xiii dai schuln dede mennes bones
Ben set to geder & rise attones
and aboue her graues stand
for þus hit schal falle in uche aland
þe xiiii dai alle þo þat liuen þan
Shullen dei bothe man child & womman
ffor þei schuln rise again
Wiþ alle þe oþur dede toforn again
þe xv day þus hit schal be tide
þat þe world schal brenne on ilk aside
and alle þe erþe wheras now we duell
Into þe furþest end of alle helle
Thus telleþ ierome off alle þese toknes xv
As he hem in ebrew bok had sene
[70v] Þe fyrste day of þo fyftene dayes
Þe see shal ryse þe booke sayes
Aboue þe hexte hylle or mountayn
Ffourty cubites large certeyne
And in his steed euen up stand
As here doþ hille upon þe land
Þe secounde day hit shal be low
Þat unneþe men shul hitte knowe
[71r] Þe þridde day hitte shal seme pleyne
And stonde euen in his course ageyne
As hit stood atte þe bygynnyngge
With outen rysyng or fallyngge
Þe ferthe day þus shal hit bee
The wondursteful fysshes of þe see
Shul come togedur and make roryng
Wondur hydous to monnes heryng
Bot what þat hit shal sygnyfie
Woot noon bot god alle myghtye
Þe fyfþe day þe se shal brynne
And wateres alle þat be þer inne
And laste fro þe sonne rysyngge
Unto hir tyme of doune goyngge
Þe syxte day shal spryng blody dew
On erthe on gras fully to shewe
Þe seuenthe day byldyng shal fall
And grete castelles and toures all
Þe eyghte day roches and stones
Shul smyte togydur all at ones
Uche to oþur shal smyte faste
So þat uche oon shul doun be caste
And euery stoon in sondry wyse
Shal be parted in þre partyese
Þe nynthe day shal erþe dyn be
Fful generally in uche contree
So greet þondur as shal be þon [n.b. “þondur”]
Was neuer sythen þe world bygon
Þe tenþe day al for to neuen
Erthe shal be maad pleyne & euen
Hulles and dales shul turned be
An be maad euen & pleyne to se
[71v] Þe elleuenth day men shul come oute [“men” inserted by caret]
Of caues of heoles ouer al aboute
As wode men þat no witte conne
And noon shal speke to oþur þonne
Þe twelfe day sterrus shal alle
And þe sonne from heuen doun falle
Þe þrittenthe day shul mennes bones
Be sette togedre alle atte ones
And upon here graues stoonde
Þis shal byfalle in uche a londe
Þe fourtenþe day alle þat lyuen þon
Shul dyge childe mon and womon
Ffor þey wiþ hem shul rise ageyne
Þat dyed byfore to ioye or peyne
Þe fyftethe day shal þus bytyde
Þe worlde shal brenne on euery syde
And þe erthe þa we inne dwelle [emend to “þar”]
Unto þe utturmaste parte of helle
Ierom telleth þes tokenes fyftene
As he in ebrue bokes had seene.
[60r] The firste day of Þe xv days [blue paraph, “primum signum”]
The see schal rise as Þe boke sais
Abouen Þe heighist of ony mountayn
Ffourtene cubitis Þis is sertayn
And euen in his stede up stonde
As hille doth here and euen londe
The seconde day it schal be lowe [blue paraph, roman numerals begin]
That unnethis men schal it knowe
The Þridde day it schal seme playn
And stonden euen in his cours agayn
As it stode atte begynnyng
Withouten risyng or fallyng
The iiiie day Þus schal it be [“e”superscripted in “iiii”]
The moste wondir fisshis of Þe see
Schul come togedir & make roryng
Ffull hidous to mannys undirstondyng
But what Þat Þay schul signyfie
Wott no man but god oonlye
[60v] The ve day Þe see schal brennyn [e superscripted; practice continues]
And Þe wateris alle Þeryn
And Þat schal laste fro Þe sunne rysyng
Til Þe tyme of Þe doun goyng
The vie day schal spryng a blody dew
On gras & treis as Þay schul schew
The viie day biggyngis schul doun falle
And grete castellis & towris withalle
The viiie day rochis & stonys
Schul stryue togedir all at onys
And eche to oÞur schal strike faste
So Þat echon schullen doun be caste
And eche ston on fele weyis [n.b. “fele”]
Schal be sondrid on fele partyis
The ixe day schal erth den be
General in euery contre
So grete erthdene as schal be Þan
Was neuer sithen Þe world began
The xe day aftir Þe nyne
The erth schal be made Þlayn & euene [note rhyme with “nyne”]
Hillis & valeyis schul turnyd be
And be made euen to Þe see
The xie day men schullen comen oute
Of cauys & hillis all abowte
And as wode men Þat no witte can
And noon schul speken to oÞre Þan
The xiie day aftir Þe sterris alle
And Þe signe [sic] fro heuen schal falle
The xiiie day schul dede mennys bonys
Be sette togedre [catchwords; missing next six leaves. See Lewis and McIntosh]
Þe firste day of þo fiftene dayus
þe see schal rise þe bok sayus
aboue þe heigthe of alle mountayne
ffourty cubitus of certayne
and euen in his stude up stonde
As hul here doth on þe londe
þe secounde day hit schal be lowe
þat unnethe men schal hit Knowe
þe thridde day hit schal seme pleyn
And stonde euen in his cours ageyn
As hit stod at þe bigynnyng
With outen risyng or fallyng
þe ferthe day þus schal hit be
þe most wondurful fissches of þe se
Schal come to gedur and make roryng
fful hidous to monnes heryng
Bot what hit schal signifie
Wot non bot god al myghtie
& wateres alle þat are þer inne
To laste fro þe sonne risyng
Til hir tyme of doun goyng
þe sexthe day schal sprynge blody dewe
On grees & trees fully to schewe
þe seuenthe day buyldyngus schul doun falle
and grete castels and toures alle
þe eigthe day roches and stones
Schul smyte to gedur alle at ones
uche to other schal smyte faste
So þat uchone schal doun be caste
and uche ston in diuerse wise
Schal be sondred in thre partise
þe nythe day schal erthe dene be
Generally in uche countre
So gret erthe dene as schal be þan
Was neuere syn þe World bigan
þe tenthe day forto neuen
Erthe schal be made pleyn & euen
hulles & valeyes schul turnd be
and be made euen forto se
þe elleuenth day men schul come oute
Of caves and of hulles aboute
As wode men þat no wit can
and non schal speke to other þan
þe twelfthe day sterres alle
and þe sonne fro heuen schal falle
þe thretthenthe day schal mennes bones
Be sette to gedur & rise at ones
and uppon hor graues stonde
þis schal bifalle in ucha londe
þe xiiii day al bat lyueth þon
Schal die child mon & Wommon
ffor þei with hem schal rise ageyn
þat diede bifore to joye or peyn
þe fiftenethe day schal þus bitide
þe world schal brenne on euery side
and þe erthe þat we yn dwelle
unto þe otmast parte of helle.
Jerom telleth þes toknes fiftene
As he in ebreu bokes had sene.
Þe forste dai of þo fiftene dais
þe se shal rise as þe bok sais
abouen þe heyte ot ich mountaigne
ffulli fourti cubites þis is certaine
and in his stede up stande
as an hye hil dos on þe lande
þe secund dai þe see shal be so lowe
þat unnethes men shal it knowe
þe thredde dai þe see shal seme plaine
and stand euene in his cours againe
as it stod at þe beginning
withouten more rising or falling
þe ferthe dai shal suche a wonder be
þe most wonderful fisches of þe see
shal com tageder & make such a romping
þat it shal be hidows to mans hering
But what þat ronnying shal signifi
non what but good almichti
þe five þe dai þe see shal brenne
and alle þe waters þat shal renne
and þat shal laste fro þe sunne risingt
þo sexte dai shal springe a blodi dew
on gres on trees as hit shal shew
þe seuenthe dai biggings doun shal falle
and grete casteles & toures with ale
þe authand dai hard roches & stones
Shal strike to gedere harde at ones
and ichon ageins oþer shal hortil fast
So þat iche ston on divers wise
Shal sundre oþer on thre partise
þe ninethe dai gret erthe dyn shal be
General in ich contre
and so gret erthe dyn as shal be þan
Was neuer herd sin þe world began
þe tenthe dai þere after to neuene
þe erthe shal be mad playn & euene
ffor hilles & valeis all turned shal be
in to plaine & made euene to see
Xi þe elleuenthe men shal come oute
of caues & holes & wenden aboute
as wode men þat no wit can
and non shal speke to oþer þan
þe twelfthe dai after þe sternes all
and þe signes fro þe heuen shal fall
þe threttenthe dai shal dede mens bones
Be sett togidere & — at onns
and abouen on hor graues stonde
þis shal befalle in ich alonde
þe fourtenthe dai all þat lives þan
Shal die child man & womman
ffor þei shul þenne rise ageyn
þat first were ded to ioyie or pein
þe fiftenethe dai þus shal be tide
all þe werld shal brenne on ich a side
And þe erthe where we now dwelle
Unto þe utter ende of all helle.
þus telles Jerome þies toknes fiftene
As he in þe bokes of hebrews had sene.
[folio 63r] The firste day of þise fiftene dais
The see shal arise as seynt ierom sais
The heighte abouen ilk a mountayne [“gh” is a yogh]
Al fully fourty cubites for certayne
And in his stede euene upstande
As an heigh wal doþ on þe lande [“gh” is a yogh]
[63v] The secounde day it shal be so lawe
That unneþis shal men þe see knawe
The thridde day god shal it so ordeyne
That þe se shal stande euene ageyne
As it was first at þe bigynnyng
Wiþouten more reisyng or fallyng
Uppon þe fourte day shal such a wonder be
The moste wonderful beestis in þe see
And [sic] shullen come togedre and make such a rownyng
That it shal be ful hidous to mannys heryng
But what so þat rownyng shal signifie
No man it wot but only god almyghttie [“gh” is a yogh]
The fifte day þe see shal brenne
And þe wattres as þei shal renne
And þat shal laste fro þe sunne arisyng
To þat tyme of þe adoun goyng
The sixte day shal sprynge a blody dewe
As graas and trees shullen it shewe
The sevenþe day biggyngis shal doun falle
Grete toures and castellis wiþ alle
The eighte day harde rochis and stones [“gh” is a yogh]
Shal smyte togedre all at ones
And eche of hem shal other adoun cast
And eche agen other hurtle fast
So þat eche stoon on dyuerse wise
Shal sundre other in dyuerse parties
The ny3ente day erthe dene shal be [3 is a yogh]
So verraily in eche cuntree
And so hidously as it shal be þanne
Was neuer noon siþ þe werld biganne
The tenþe day þeraftir to nempne [sic]
The erthe shal be maad pleyn & euene
Ffor hillis and valleies shal turned be
Al pleyn and euene uppon to see
The enleuenþe day men shal come out
Of holis and caues and wenden about
As wode men þat no wytt ne can
And noon shal speke to other þan
The twelfþe day þeraftir þe sterris alle
And þe signes fram heuene shullen falle
[64r] The thryttenþe day shal dede mennys bones
Ben sett togedre and þei shal rise all at ones
And abouen uppon her graues stande
Ffor þus it shal falle in ilk a lande
The fourtenþe day all þo þat lyuen þan
Shal deie boþe man child and woman
Ffor þei shal wiþ hem rise agayn
That toforn were dede & goo to ioie or to payn
The fiftene day þus it shal bityde
That þe werld shal brenne on ilk a side
And al þis erthe where as now we dwelle
Into þe fertheste ende of al helle
Thus telliþ Ierom of þise tokenes fiftene
As he hem in Ebreus bokis hadde sene
þe first day of þe fiftene dayes
þe see schal rise als þe boke sayes
abouen þe heght of ilk mountayne
ffourty cubyts for sothe certayne
and euen in his stede upstande
als hilles her dose on þe lande
þe secund day it schal be law
þat unnethes men schal it know
þe thrid day schal it seme playne
& stande euen in his course agayne
Als it stode at þe begynnyng
With outen rising or fallyng
þe furth þus schal it be
þe moste wundurful fisshes in þe see
schal come togedur & make romyng
fful hidouse unto mannes heryng
What þat it schal signify
Wote noman bot god all myghty
þe fyft day þe see schal bryn
& þe waturs als þai ryn
& þat schal laste fro þe soun risyng
Unto þe tyme of þe doun gangyng
þe sext day schal spring a blody dew
On gresse & trees als þan schal schew
þe seuent day schal biggyngs doun fall
and grete castels & toures with alle
þe aght day roches & stones
schal strike to gedur alle at ones
ilkone til other schal strike faste
So þat þan schal be alle down caste
and ilk ston on diverse wysc
Schal be sundurd in thre partyse
þe nyent day schal erthe dyn be
Generally in ilk cuntre
So grete erthe dyn als schal be þan
Was neuer syn þe worlde began
þe tent day after to neuen
þe erthe schal be made playne & euen
hilles & valeys schal turned be
& be made alle euen to se
þe elleuen day men schal come oute
of cafes & of holes aboute
als wodemen þat no witt can
& none schal speke til oþer þan
þe twelft day after þe sternes alle
& þe signes fro þe heuen schal falle
þe thrittend day schal ded mans bones
Be sett to gedur & ryse at ones
& upon þair groues stande
þis schal befalle in ilk lande
þe fourtenday alle þat lyfes þan
schal dye childe man & woma [sic]
ffor þai with þaim schal ryse agayn
þat dyed before til ioy or payne
þe fyftene day schal þus be tide
þe world schal brenne on ilk a syde
& þe erthe qwhere we now duell
Unto þe utturst ende of alle hell
þus telles Jerome þise tokens fyftene
als he in Ebrew bokes hade sene.
The furste day of the xv dayes
The see schal a ryse þus þe boke sayes
a bowe the heghyst mountayne
fourty cubytys al yn certayne
and evend stylle yn the stede stonde
as doth here hylles yn the londe
The secunde day hit schal be lowe
That unnethe men schulle hit knowe
The thrydde day hyt schal seme playne
and stonde yn hys cors agayne
as hyt stode at the begynnyng
withoute rysyng other fallyng
The furde day gyt schal hyt be
most wonder þat fysches of the sea
schal come to gedere & make gronyng
fful dulful on to manys heryng
bote wat hit schal synefye
wote no man bot god onlye
The u day the see schal brenne
and al the water þat ys þer ynne
and þat schal leste fro sonne rysyng
unto the tyme of doun goyng
The syxte day schal sprynge blod dew
in gras treis as hy wol shewe
The sovethe day howsyng schul doun falle
and grete castelys & townys alle
The viii day rochys & stonys
Schulle smyte to gederys al atonys
Every other schal smyte fast
So þat al shal be adoun cast.
And every stone yn dyuerse wyse
Schal be departed in dyuerse partyse
The ix day schal gerthe quaque be
Oueral y leke yn euery contre
So gret erthe quakyng as schal be than
Was neuer syþ the world began
The x day after to mene
The erthe schal be playne & euene
hull & valyse schal turnyd be
And be maked al evene yn to þe see
The xi day men schul come out
Of caves & of clowdys about
as wode men þat no good can
And none schal speke to oþer than
The xii day the styrres alle
and sygnes adoun fro heuene schal falle
The xiii day schal dede bonys
Be set to geder & ryse at tonys
and opyn the berellys stonde
Thys schal be falle yn euery londe
The xiiii day ho þat leveth þan
Schul day chyld woman & man
for hy wyth hem schul ryse agene
That day before to ioye oþer to payne
The xv day schal thys betydd
The world schal brenne yn euery syde
and al erthe here there we dwelle
Unto the dupe pyt of helle.
Thus telleth Sint Ierom tokenys xv
That he yn a boke of ebrew had sene
Þe furst day of þe fyftene dayes
þe see schal ryse as þe bok sayes
aboue þe heyhest of ony mounteyne
ffourtene cubitis þis is certeyne
and euene in his stede up stande
as lyl doth here and euen lande
þe secunde day it schal be lowe
þat on
þe thredde day schal it seme pleyne
and stonden euen in his chours ageyne
as it stode atte þe begynyng
with outen rysyng or flouyng
þe ferthe day þus schal it be
þe moste wonder þe fyschis of þe see
schun comen to geder and maken rognyng
fful hetous to menns under stondyng
Wat pei schuln sygnefye
Woth no man buth got onlye
þe fyfte day þe see shal bren
And þe water al þerin
and þat schal leste fro þe sunne rysyng
til þe tyme of þe doun goyng
þe sexte day schal spryngen a blody dew
on gres on tres as þei schuln schew
þe seuente day byggynges schuln doun falle
and grete casteles and touris with alle
þe viii day roches and stones
schun stroken to geder alle atones
So þat iche on doun schal be caste
and iche a ston on fele wyse
schal ben sundered on fele partise
þe ix day schal erthe den be
Oueral in iche cunctre
So gret erthe den as schal be þan
Was neuer sythyn þe world be gan
þe x day after þe neuen
þe erthe schal be made pleyn & euen
hillis and valeys schun turned be
and ben mad euen to þe see
On þe xi day men schun come oute
of caues and hillis aboute
and as wode men þat no witt can
and non schal speken to other þan
þe xii day after þe sterris alle
and þe syngne fro heune schal falle
þe xiii day schun dede menns bonys
Be fet to gedere and rysen attonis
and up on here grvies stande
þis schal be fallen in iche a lande
þe xiiii day alle þat leuen þan
Schun deyen bothe man & woman
ffor with hem schuln rysen agayn
þat deyeden beforn to ioie or to payn
þe fyftene day schal be tyde
þe werld schal brenne on iche a syde
ffro þe erthe þer we now dwelle
to þe netherest ende of helle
þus tellit Ierom þese tokenes xv
as he in ebru bokes had sene.
ffryst day of þe fyftene days
þe se shal ryse as þe bok says
Abouen þe hyest of ilk a mountayne
ffourty cobytys for certayne
and euene in his stede up stande
As hyl doth her in euery lande
þe secunde day it schal be lowe
þat unnethe men schal it knowe
þe thredde day shal it seme playne
And stande euene in his cours agayne
As it stod at þe begynnyng
With outen rysyn or fallyng
Þe forthe day þus shal it be
þe most wondyr fysch of þe se
schal come to gedere & makyn rounyngt
fful hedous on to manys heryngt
Wat þat þay schal signefeye
Wot no man but god onlye
Þe fyfte day þe se schal brenne
And þe water al þerinne
And þat schal leste fro þe sunne rysyng
On to þe tyme of doun goyngt
Þe sexte day schal spreng blody dew
On gres & tres as þei shal shew
Þe seuent day byggyngis shal doun falle
and gret castelles & tours with alle
þe eyahte day roches & stones
schal stryue to geder al at ones
and ilkune shal doun be cast
[missing line]
and ilk a stond on sere wyse
schal be sondyr on thre partye [sic]
Þe nyent daye schal erdedene be
General in ilk a countre
So gret erdedene as schal be þan
was neuere syn þe ward [sic] be gan
Þe tende day after to neuen
þe erde schal be mad pleyn & euen
hilles & valleys schal turned be
and be mad euen up on to se
Þe elleuent day men schal come oute
of cauys and of hilles aboute
and alle wode men þat no good can
and none schal speke tyl other þan
Þe thredtende day schul dede menys bonys
Be set to geder & rysen at honys
and upon her graues stande
þis schal befalle in ilke a lande
Þe fourtende day al þat leuyth þan
schal deye man and wymman
ffor with hem schal ryse agayn
þat deyed be forn to ioye or payn
Þe fyftende day schal betyde
þe werd [sic] schal brennyn on ilke a syde
And þe erde þer we now dwelle
Un to þe utterest ende of helle
þus tellith Ierom þis toknes fyftene
As he in ebrw [sic] bokys hadde sene.
Þe ferste of þe xv dayzes
þe se schal rise as þe bok seys
aboue þe heyzeste mownteyne
ffulle xl cubitis certeyne
and in his stede euene up stonde
as an hey hel doþ on þe londe
þe ii day þe se schal be so lowe
þat unneþis eny man schal it knowe
þe þridde day þe se schal seme pleyn
and stonde euene in his cours ageyn
as it stod at þe bygynnyg [sic]
wiþ oute eny more rysyng or lowyng
þe ferþe day schal swich a wondir be
þat þe moste wondirful fysschis of þe se
Schul come to gedir & make rownyg [sic]
þat it schal be hidon to mannys heryng
But what þe rownyng schal sygnefye
No man may wete but god almygtye
þe fifþe day þe se schal brenne
and alle þe waterys þat a wher renne
and þat schal laste fro þe sunne rysyng
to þe tyme of þe sunnys doun goyng
þe sixte day schal sprynge a blody dew
On gres on tre as it schal schew
þe vii day biggyngys doun schul falle
and grete castelis & townys wiþ alle
þe viii day grete rochis stonys
Schul strike to gedur al atonys
and echon of hem schal oþer doun caste
and ech wiþ oþer hurtle faste
So þat ech ston in dyuerse wyse
Schal sondur oþer on þre partyse
þe neyþe day a gret erþedene schal be
Generaly in ech a cuntre
and so gret erþedene as schal be þenne
Was neuere herd seþe þe world beganne
þe tenþe day þerafter to neuyn
Schal come & wynd fro heuyn
þat hillys & valeyis turnyd schul be
In to pleyn & mad euen to þe se
þe elleuenþe day men schul come oute
of caues & hillis & weude aboute
as wode men þat no wit can
and non schal speke to þat oþer þan
þe tuelfþe day after þe sterris alle
and þe sygnys fro hefne schul falle
þe þryttenþe day schulle dede menns bonys
Ben set to gedere & ryse atonys
and up on here graues stonde
þis schal be falle in ech a londe
þe fortenþe day alle þat leuen þanne
schul deyze child man & womanne
ffor þey schul wiþ hem ryse ageyn
þat byfore were dede to ioyze or to peyn
þe fyftenþe day þus schal betyde
þe world schal brenne on ech a syde
and þe erþe þere we dwelle
In to þe utterr ende of alle helle
þus tellith Ierom þese toknes fyftene
as he in þe book of hebrews hath sene
Þe furste of þe fiftene days
þe see schal ryse as þe boke says
aboue þe eyre of the montayne
fully fourty cubitus certayne
and in his stede euene upstonde
as an hey hulle doþ on þe londe
þe secunde day þe see schal be also lowe
þat unneþe eny man schal hit knowe
þe þridde day þe see schal seme playn
and stonde in hys cours euene agayn
as hyt stode furst ate þe bygynnynge
withoute more rysynge or fallynge
þe ferþe suche a wondere schal be
þe moste wonderfulle fyschys of þe see
schule rome to gedere and make a romynge
þat hyt ys hydous to manns herynge
Bote what þat romynge doþ sygnyfye
No man may wyte bute god almygtie
þe fifte day þe see schal brenne [b cancelled by dots]
and þat scal laste al þe waters as þei schule renne
and þat schal laste fro þe sonne rysynge
To þe tyme of þe sonne down goyunge
þe syxte day schal sprynge a blody dewe
On gras on tree as hit schal schewe
þe seuenþe day byggyngs schal down falle
and grete castlus and tours with alle
þe e[?]te day grete roches and stones
schule stryke to gedere alle at ones
and eche one of hem schal doun oþere caste
and eche one ageyn oþere tulte faste
So þat eche a stone in dyuers wyse
schal smyte oþere on þre partyse
þe nynþe ys þe grete erþe dene þat schal be
Generale in eche a contre
and so grete erþe dyne as schal be þan
was neuere herde here seþe þe world bygan
þe tenþe day þereaftur to nemene
schal come a wynd fro heuene
þat hulles and vales turnd schule be
Into pleyn and made euene to se
þe elleuenþe day men schule come oute
of caues and holes and wende aboute
as wode men þat no wyt can
and none schal speke to oþere þan
þe twelfþe day aftur þe sterres alle
and þe sygnes fro heuene schule falle
þe þrettene day schule dede men bons
Be sete to gedere and ryse at onns
and upon here graues stonde alle
þis schal byfore in eche a lond falle
þe fourtene day alle þat lyueþ þan
schule deye chyld and man & woman
ffor þei schulde a ryse with hem agayn
þat byfore were dede to ioye or to payn
þe fyftene day schal þus bytyde
þe world schal brenne on eche a syde
and þe erþe þere þere nowe we dwelle
unto þe utterer ende of helle
þus telleþ uerome þe tokenes fyftene
as he in þe bokus of þe hebreus haþ sene.
þe first day of þe fiftene dayes
þe see shal rise þe booke sayes
aboue þe hygest cop [sic] of any mounteyn
ffourty cubites þat is certeyn
and euene yn his stede upstonde
as an hille here dooþ uppon þe londe
þe second day hit shal be so lowe
þat men unneþe shullen hit knowe
þe þridde day hit shal seme pleyne
and stonde euene yn his cours ageyne
as hit stoode at þe bygynnyng
wiþ oute risyng or fallyng
þe ferþe day þus shal hit be
þat þe grete fisshes of þe se
shul come to gider & make roryng
fful hidouse to mannys heeryng
But what hit shal signyfye
Woot noon but god almygtye
þe fifte day þe se shal brynne
and alle þe watris þat are þerynne
and þat shal last fro þe sonne rysyng
Til here tyme off doun goyng
þe sixte day shal spryng blody dewe
on grasse and trees deolfully to shewe
þe seuenþe day bildynges shul doun falle
and grete castellis and tourys alle
þe eygte day rochis and stonys
shul smyte to gider alle at onys
uche to oþir shal smyte fast
so þat vchone shal be doun cast
and uche a stone yn dyuerse wyse
shal be brokyn yn þre partyse
þe nynþe day shal erþe quak be
Generally yn uche contree
So grete dyn as shal be þan
was neuer seþ þe world bigan
þe tenþe day forto neuene
al þe erþe shal be made pleyn & euene
hulles and valeyes shul turnyd bem
and be made euene alle men to se
þe elleuenþe day men shul se out
off caues and hilles alle about
As wode men þat no goode can
and noon shal speke to oþir þan
þe twelþe day sterrys alle
and þe sonne from heuen shal falle
þe þrittenþe day shul mennys bonys
Be sette to gyder alle atte onys
and uppon her graues stonde
þis shal bifalle in erþe londe
þe fourtenþe day al þat leuen þan
shul dyze child man & woman
ffor þey wiþ hem shul rise agayn
þat dyzed bifore to ioye or payn
þe fiftenþe day shal þis bityde
þe world shal brenne on uche side
and þe erþe þat we in dwelle
unto þe utmest party of helle
Ierom telliþ þise toknes fiftene
as he yn hebrew bookis had hem sene.
These firste of þese fiftene dayes
þe ses shal ryse as seynt Ierome says
aboue þe heygte of euery mountayne
fful fourty cubytes he saiþ certayne
Primu prodigiu ante diem iucy
and in hese stede euen up rygt stande
as an heyge wall aboue þe lande
The seconde day it shall be so lowe
þat unneþe any man þe see schal knowe
The þredde day god shal so ordayne
þat þe see shall stande euene agayne
as it was firste at þe begynnyng
with oute any more rysynge or fallynge
The iiii day shal most wondres bee
þe moste wonderfull bestes in þe see
Shul come to gedre and make seche rorynge
þat it shal be grysely to mannes herynge
Bot what þat hydous noyce shal signyfye
no man ne woote but god almyghty
The V day alse þe see shall brenne
and þe waters als þay shall renne
and þat shall laste fro þe sunne rysynge
unto þe tyme of here doune gooynge
The vi day shal sprynge a rede blody dew[?]e
On grees and trees þat openly shal shewe
The vii day byldynges shall doun fall
as grete castelles and towres wiþ all
The viii day harde roches and stones
Shul bete to gedre alle at ones
and eueryche oþer schul doun caste
agayne oþer bete full faste
So þat euery stone on dyuers wyse
shul forfuiyten oþer on þre partyse
þe ix day þe erde dyne shall bee
so verrayly and huge in euery countree
so grete and wonderfull as shal be þan
was neuer [?]it herde syþe þe werld began
The x after þat to neuene
þe erþe shal be made pleyne and euene
ffor hylles and valeys shul turned bee
alle in to pleyne as euene as is þe see
The xi day men shal come oute
of holes and saues [sic] and wende aboute
as wood men þat haþ no wyt ne can
and none shall speke to oþer þan
and þe sygnes of heuene shul doun falle
Be sette to gedre and ryse alle at onys
and aboue up on heire graues shul stonde
for þus it shall fare in euery londe
The xiiii day alle þat be leuynge þan
þay shal deye man chylde and woman
ffor þai shal þan up ryse agayne
þat before were dede to ioye or payne
The xv day thys shall betyde
alle þe werld shal brenne on every syde
and alle þis erde where we now dwell
Into þe utterest poynte of all hell
Thus telleþ ierome þese tokenes fiftene
as he in þe book of hebreos haþ sene.

[folio 65v] Þe ferste day of þe fyftene dayes
Þe see schal ryse as þe bok sayes
Aboue þe toppe of eueryche Monteyne
Fourty cubytes in certeyne

[66r, and straight bracket turn into a wavy bracket, contrary to usual practice but it does happen] With outen rysyng oþer doun fallyng

Generally in eueryche . . . contre

Þe x day after for to nemene
Þe erthe schal be mad playn and euene

[66v] Of holes and caues aboute
As wode men þat no wyt can

Þe xii day after þe sterres alle
And þe sonne from heuene schal falle
Þe xiii day schal dede men bones
Be set to gyder and ryse atones
And on her graues þei schal up stande
Þis schal betyde in eueryche lande
Þe xiiii day whose [sic] lyueþ þanne
Schal dye boþe chyld man and wommanne
For þei with hem schal rysen ageyne
Þat dyeden byfore to ioye or payne
Þe xv day schal þus betyde
Þe world schal brenne on eueryche syde
And þe erthe here whare we duelle
Unto þe uttemost part of al helle
Þus telleþ Ierome toknes fyftene
As he in hebru bokes hadde hem sene
The furst day of þe fyften dayes
The schal ryse þes bokes þus sayþes
aboue þe heygest moutayne
xl cubytes all certayne
and euen stylle in stede stonde
as doþ her hylles in þes londs
The second day yt schal be lowe
That unneþes men schulleþes yt knowe
The þrydde day yt schal seme playne
and stond euen in hor cors ageyne
as yt stoud ate þes bygynnyng
with oute oþer rysyng oþer fallyng
The furþes [?]utte schal yt be
most wondre þat fysches of þes se
Schulleþ cum togedyr and make gronyng
wel dusefid unto mannys huyryng
Bote what yt schal signifye
Wote no man bote god onelye
The fyfte day þe see schal brenne
and all þe watar [?] þat ys þer inne
and þat schal laste fram sunne rysyng
Unto þes tyme of doune goyng
The syxte day schal spring blode dewe
In gras in trees as hy schulleþ doun falle
and gret castells and toures withalle
The egþes day roches and stones
Schulleþ smyte to gedur alle ate ones
echone oþer schal smyte faste
So þat echone schal be doun caste
and euery stone in dyuers wyse
Schal be departyd in dyuers partyes
The nygþes day in [?] schal erþes dyne be
Generallyche in uche contree
So grete erþes dyne as schal be þanne
Was neuer syþes þe world bygan
The teþes day after to neuen
The erþe schal be made playne & euen
hyll and valeyis schulleþes turnyd be
and be made all euen to þes se
The elleueþes day men schulleþes cume oute
of cauys and of cloudes aboute
as wode men þat no wytte canne
and none schulleþes speke to oder þanne
The twelue day þe sterrys alle
and signes from heuen a donn schulleþes fall
The þryttend day schulleþes ded bones
Be fette to geder and aryse ate ones
and upon þer buryels stonde
Thus scal befall in eche londe
The fourtend day hy þat lyueþes þanne
for hy with hom schulleþes ryse agen
That deyzed before oþer to payne
The fyfteþes day schal þus bytyde
The world schal brenne in eche syde
and erþes here þer we dwell
unto þes pure putt of hell
Thus telleþes seynt ierome toknysse fyftene
That he in ebreu bokes had ysene.
þe first of þes fiftene daies
þe see shal rise as seint ierome sais
aboue þe hygt of eche mountaigne [sic]
as fourty cubites for certayne
and in his stede euene up stonde
as an hiewalle in þe londe
þe seconde day hit shal be so lowe
þat unneþe any man þe see shal knowe
The þridde day god shal do ordayne
þat þe see stal [sic] stonde euene agayne
as hit was fyrst atte þe bygynnyng
Wiþ out more risyng or fallyng
The þridde day shal most wondres be
þe most wonderful bestes in þe see
Shal come togadre and make siche romyng
þat hit shal be hidone to mannes heryng
Bote what romyng shal signefye
No man wote bote god almygty
The fyþe day þe se shall brenne
as þe waters alle shal þei renne
and þat shal last fro þe sonne rsing [sic]
Tille þe waters alle shal þei renne
and þat shal last fro þe sonne rsing [sic]
Tille þe tyme þe sonne is doin goyng
The sixe day shall spryng a blody dewe
on gras and tree þan shal hit shewe
The vii day buldynges shal doun falle
and grete castell and toures wiþ alle
The eygte day roches & stones
Shal stryke doun alle atones
and eche of hem shal oþer doun caste
and agen oþer hurtle fast.
So þat eche stone in diuerse wise
Shal clefe oþer in dinerse partice [sic]
þe nygt day gret þonder shal be
So verray in eche countre
and so myche shal be þan
was neuer herde siþ þe worlde bygan
The tenþe day after to nemepne
þe erþe shal be made playn and euene
ffor hilles and valeys shal turned be
alle into plain and euene to se
The xi day men shul come oute
of holes and caues and wende al aboute
as wode men þat no witte can
and non shal speke to oþer þan
The xii day after þe sterres alle
and þe signes fro heuene shul falle
The xiii day shul dede men bonys
Be sette togadre and rise alle attonys
and aboue uppon here graues stonde
for þus shal falle in eche londe
The fourteþe day alle þat lyue þan
Shal dey man childe and woman
ffor thei shul wiei shul wiþ hem rise agayne
þat byfore were dede to ioy or payne
The fifteþe day þis shalle by tyde
The worlde shalle brenne in eche a side
and þe erþe where we now dwelle
Unto þe outer ende of alle helle.
þus telleþ Ierome þes toknes fiftene
as he in hebreus bokes haþ sene.
þe ferst of þes fiftene dayes
þe se schal rise as þe book saies
aboue þe heiest of any montayn
ffully fourty cupites certeyn
and his stede euen up stonde
as an hye hille doþ in the londe
þe secunde day þe se schal be low
þat unneþes any man schal hit know
Þe þridde day þe se schal seme pleyn
and stonde euen in his cours ageyn
as hit stode ferst at hys bygynnynge
wiþoute more risynge or fallynge
þe ferthe day schal suche a wonder be
þat þe most wonderfull fisches of þe se
Shull come to gedre & make a rorynge
þat hit schal be hydous to mannes heryng
But what þat roryng schal sygnifie
No man may wit but god almyghty
þe fift day þe se schal brenne
and alle þe watres þey schal renne
and þat schal last fro þe sonne risynge
Tyl þe tyme of þe sonne adoun goynge
þe sexte day schal sprynge a blody dewe
On gres & on tres as hit schal schewe
þe seueneþ day bigynges doun doun schal fall
and grete castellys & toures with all
þe eght day grete roches & stones
Shull fygt to gedre all at ones
and echon of hem schull foun oþer cast
and echon agen oþer schal hortel fast
So þat ech a stone yn dyuers wyse
Schal to brest oþer in þre partise
þe nyeth day gret erthe den schal be
generaly in eche a londe & in ech a contre
and so gret erthe dynne þan schal be
Siþ þe worlde be gan was neuer herde ne se
þe tenþe day þere after euens
Schal come a wynde fram heuen
þat hilles & valeis turned schull be
In to pleyn & made euen to se
þe eleueth day men schulle come out
of caues & holes & wende about
as wode men þat no witte ne kan
and nons schal speke to oþer þan
þe twelneth day after þe sterres all
and þe synge fro heuens schull fall
þe þrittene day schull ded men bones
be sette to gedre & ryse atones
and up on her graues stonde
þys schal by fall yn ech a londe
þe fourteneþ day all þat lyueþ þan
Schulle dye childe and woman
for þey sculle [sic] with hem rise ageyne
þat bi for were ded to ioy or peyne
þe XV day þus schal be tyde
þe world schal brenne on ech a side
and þe utterest ende of hell
þus telleþ Ierome þes toknesse XV
as yn þe boke of hebreeues haþ sens.

Unto þe owtrest þet of helle
Þus telleþ Ierome þus tokenys xv.

þe furst day of þe fiftene dayus
þe see schal rise þe boke sayus
a boue þe heigt of al mountayne
ffourti cubites of certayne
& in his stede up fonde
As her doigt up on þe londe
þe secunde day it schal be lowe
þt unneþe men schal it knowe
þe þridde day schal seme playn
& stonde euene in his cours agayn
As it stode at þe begynnyng
Wight oute risyng or fallyng
þe fertþe day þus schal it be
þe most wonndirful þe feisches of þe see
Schul come to gedur & make roring
fful hidous to mannes heryng

Wot non but god almittie
þe fifte day þe se schal brenne
& waters alle þat are þer inne

Til hir tyme of doun goyng
þe sixte day schal springe blodi dew
On gres & trees fulli to schewe
þe sefuente day byldynges scho[sic]l doun falle
& gret castellis & toures alle

Schul smigte to gedur al at ones
uche to oþur smite schal faste
So þat vchons doun schal be cast
& uche ston in diuerse wise
schal be sundred in þre partise
þe nynt day schal erþe den be
Generali in uche cuntre
