Was neuer syn þe world be gan
Tent day for to neuen
Erþe schal be mad pleyn & euen
hillis & valeis schul turned be
& be mad euen for to se
þe eleuent day men schol com oute
of caues & of holes aboute
Os wod men þat no wit can
& non schal speke to oþer þan
þe tuelfte day sterris alle
& þe sonne fro heuene schol falle
þe þredtent day schal mennes bones
Be segt to gedir & rise a tones
& up on her graues stonde
þis schal be falle in vche a londe
þe fourtent day alle þat leues þan
schal di[?]e child man & womman
ffor þi with hem schal rise a geine
þe fiftent day schal þus be tide
þe world schal brenne on eueri side
& þe erþe þat we in duelle
unto þe utturest part of helle
Ierom tellugt þe tokennis fiftene
As he in ebru bokes had sene
þe first of þese xv days
þe see sal rise als seynt Ierome says
a bouen þe higt of ilk mountayne
fful fourty cubites als for certayne
and in his stede euen up stande
als ane heye walle on þe land
þe secund day it sal be so lowe
þat unneþes sal men þe see knawe
þe þridde day god sal so ordayne
þat þe see sal stand euen agayne
als it was first at þe begynnyng
Wiþ outen more risyng or fallyng
þe fourthe day sal moste wondres be
þe moste wonderful bestes in þe see
Sal come to gider & make swilk rouryng
þat it sal be hidus to mans heryng
Bot qwat þat rouryng sal signyfy
No man wate bot god almigty
þe v day þe see sal bryn
and þe watres als þai sal ryn
and þat sal laste fro þe sone risyng
Til þe tyme of þe son doun goyng
þe vi day sal spring a blody dewe
On gres & trees þat sal it schewe
þe vii day byggynges sal doun falle
and grete castelles & toures wiþ alle
þe viii day hard roches & stones
sal strike to gider alle at ones
and ilkone of þam sal oþer doun caste
and agayne oþer hortel fast
So þat ilk a stone on diuers wise
Sal sunder oþer in þre partyse
þe ix day erthe dyn sal be
So verayly in ilk cuntre
and so mikel als sal be þan
Was neuer herd sen þe world began
þe x day þare after to neuen
þe erþe sal be made playne & euen
ffor hilles & valeys sal turned be
alle in to playne & euen to se
þe xi day men sal come oute
of holes & caues & wende aboute
als wode men þat no witt can
and none sal speke to oþer þan
þe xii day efter þe sternes alle
and þe signes fr[?] þe heuen sal falle
þe xiii day sal deade mens bones
Be sette to gider & rise at ones
and bouen a pon þair graues stand
ffor þus sal falle in ilk a land
þe xiiii day alle þat lyues þan
sal deye man childe & woman
ffor þai sal wiþ þam rise agayne
þat befor ware deade to ioy or payne
þe xv day þis sal betyde
þe world sal bryn on ilk a syde
and þe erthe qware we now dwelle
Un to þe utter ende of alle helle
þus telles Ierome þese tokenes fiftene
als he in hebreus bokes has sene.
The fyrst[?] day of þo fyftene days
þe se xal ryson as þe bok says
abouen þe heyeth of euerych mountayne
ffourty cubytes for sertayne
& euene ryth up in stede stande
as her doth tour & tres in þis lande
The secunde day it xal falle lowe
þat anethys men xal it knowe
The thrydde day it xal seme al pleyn
& stondyn euen in his cours ageyn
as it stod at þe begynnyng
With owtyn rysyng or elles fallyng
The ferthe day thus xal it be
þe most wondyrful fyschys of þe se
xul come to gedyr & make rowyng
fful hedous to mannys heryng
but what yt xal sygnefy
ne wot non but gret god only
The vte day þe se xal brenne
& alle þe watrys þat are þer inne
& þat xal lestyn fro sunne rysyng
In to þat tyme of doun gangyng
The sexte day xal sprynge a blody dewe
On gres on tres as hee xal schewe
Tho vii day cytes & tounys & cros xal doun falle
& grete casteles & towres with alle
The viii day rocchys & stones
xal fyth to geder alle atones
Ich of hem to oþer xal fyth faste
þat ich of hem xal othyr doun caste
and iche ston in sundry wyse
schal brekyn oþer in dyuerse partyse
Tho ix day sal Erthedene be
Generaly in euerych lond & contre
So gret ethesdene [sic] as xal be þan
Was neuere sythen þis werd [sic] be gan
The x day aftyr þan euene [sic]
al erthe xal be mad pleyn & Euene
hylles & valeys al turnyd be
& ben mad alle euene to se
The xi day xal come owthe
ffro caues & cragges abowte
as deede men þat no wyth ne can
& non of hem to oþer xal spekyn þan
The xii aftyr þe sterres alle
& othyr sygnes fro heuene xal falle
The xiii day xal dede mennys bones
Ben set to geder & rysen alle atones
& up on here graues þan stande
þus it xal befalle in ych a lande
The xiiii day alle þat leuys þan
xal deyen man chyld & woman
& þei with othyr xal ryse ageyn
þat deyden be forn & were in ioye or payn
The xvte day xal thus be tyde
al þe werd xal brenne on ich a syde
& þe Erde where we now dwelle
In to þe netherest pyt of helle
Thus telles Ierom tokenes xvte
as he in Ebrues books had hem sene
þe firste day of fiftene dayes
þe see schal rise as þe bok saies
Aboue þe hegþe of the hiest mountayne
ffourty cubitus at certayne
And euene in his stude stonde
As hul here doþ ouer þe lowe lande
þe iiae day þese schalle so lowe þat unneþe
men schul hyt knowe
þe iii day at certayn Euene in his cours agayn
Schal it stonde as it stod atte bigynnyng
Wiþ outen risyng or fallyng
þe furþe day þus schal hit be
þe most wonderful fysches of see
Schul come togeder & make a roryng
fful hidous & gastly to monnes heryng
But what hit schal signifye
Wot non but god almigtye
þe fyfþe day þe se schal brenne
And wattres alle þat are þerinne
To laste fro þe sunne risyng
Til tyme of hire doun goyng
þe sixte day schal sprynge a blody dewe
On gres & trees fully to schewe
þe seuenþe day schul bildinges doun falle
And grete casteles & toures alle
þe egteþe day boþe roches & Stones
Schul smyte togeder alle at ones
And uche to oþer schal smyte so faste
þat ucon schal adoun be caste
And uche ston in diuers wyse
Schal be sondred in þre partise
þe nyenþe day schal siche erþe dene be
Generaly in uche cuntre
þat so gret erþe dene as schal be þan
Was neuer seyen [sic] siþ þe world bigan
þe tenþe day as I say þee
Al erþe pleyn & euene schal maad be
hulles & valeys schul turned ben
And al schalle euene on to sen
þe elleuenþe day men schul come oute
of caues & of holes aboute
As wodde men þat no wyt can
And non schal speke to oþer þan
þe twolþe [sic] day sterres alle
And þe sunne fro heuene schul falle
þe þrettenþe day schal mennes bones
Be set togedre [sic] & rise at ones
And upon her graues stonde
þis schal falle in eueryche londe
þe fourtenþe dai alle þat lyuen þan
Schul die chil [sic] man & womman
ffor þei schul with hem rise ageyne
þat diede tofore to ioye or peyne
þe world al aboute þat is so wyde
Wiþ fuyr schal brenne on euery side
And þe erþe þat we inne dwelle
To þe [?]temast part of helle
Ierom telleþ þese tokenes fyftene
As he in ebreus bokes hade hem sene
þe first day of þas fyftene days
þe see sall ryse als þe buke says
abouen þe heght of ilka mountayn
fully fourty cubytes certayn
and in his sted euen up stand
als ane hegh hill rose on þe land
þe secund day þe see sall be swa law
þat unnethes men sall it knaw
þe thred day þe see sall seme playne
and stand euen in his cours agayne
als it stode first at þe bigynyng
with outen mare rysyng or fallyng
þe ferth day sall swilk a wonder be
þe most wonderfull fysshes of þe see
sall com togyder & mak swilk rouryng
þat it sall be hydus till mans heryng
But what þat romiyng [sic] sall signify
Na man may witt bot god all myghty
þe fyft day þe see sall brynne
and all þe waters als þai sall rynne
and þat sall last fra þe son rysyng
Till þe tyme of þe son doune gangyng
þe sext day sall spryng a blody dew
On grysse & tresse als it sall shew
þe seuend day byggynges sall fall
and gret castels & toures with all
þe eghtend day hard roches & stanes
Sall stryke togyder all at anes
and ilkane of þam sall other doun cast
and ilk ane ogayn other hortell fast
swa þat ilk a stane on diuerse wise
sall sonder other in thre partyse
þe neghend day gret erthdyn sall be
Generally in ilk a contre
and swa gret erthdyn als sall be þan
was neuer hard sen þe world bi gan
þe tend day þare eff to neuen
þe erth sall be made playn & euen
For hilles & valleis sall turned be
in tyll playn & made euen to se
þe elleuend day men sall com out
of caues & holes & wend obout [sic]
als wod men þat na witt kan
and nane sall spek till other þan
þe twelft day after þe sternes all
and þe signes fra þe heuen sall fall
þe thretend day sall ded men banes
Be sett to gyder & ryse all at anes
and obouen on þarr graues stand
þis sall bifall in ilk a land
þe fourtend day all þat lyfes þan
sall deghe child man & woman
for þai sall with þam ryse ogayne
þat bifore war ded outher till ioye or payn
þe fyftend day þus sall be tyde
all þe world sall bryn on ilk syde
And þe erth whare we now dwell
untill þe utter end of all hell
þus telles Ierom þir [sic] takens fyftene
als he in þe bokes of Ebreus has sene.
the first day of þese XV dayis
the see shal ryse seynt Ierome sais
a boue þe higest of ilk a mounten
fully xl cubitis certeyn
and in his stede euen stande
as an hige wall dois on þe lande
the second day it shal be so low
that unnethe men shal þe se knowe
the þird day God shal so ordeyn
that þe see shal stond even agayn
as it was first atte begynnyng
without more risyng or fallyng
the forthe day shall swilk a wondir be
The most wondirful bestis on to se
Shall come to gidur & make suche a roryng
That it shall be gri[?]ly to mannys hereyng
But what þat roryng shal signify
no man woot but God almygty
The fifft day þe see shall bren
The waters als shall ren
and þat shal last fro þe son risyng
Tyl þe tym þe son go to restyng
The sixt day shall spryng a blody dewe
On gres & trees þat shal hym shewe
The seventhe day shal grete beldingis doun fall
and grete castellis & townys with all
The viii day grete rochis & stones
Shull smyt to gidurs al attonys
and eche of hem shal othir doun cast
and ilkon ayeyn othir huntil fast
So that ilk a ston in diuers wyse
Shall sandren on thre parties
The ix day þe erthe quake shal be
So outraye in ilk a contre
and so mykill as shal be than
Was neuer seen sithens þe world began
The x day þer afrur to [?]euen
The erthe shal be made pleyn & even
ffor hillis & valeyis shul turned be
al unto pleyn and even cuntre
The xi day men shal com oute
of hillis & cavis & wondu aboute
as wood men þat no good can
and noon shal speke til othir than
The xii day the sterris all
and the signes fro heuen shal fall
The xiii day shal dede mennys bonys
Be sette gidurs & rise al attonys
The xiiii day al þat lyuen than
Shal dye man child & womman
ffor þey shal rise withe þem agayn
That be fore were dede to ioy or peyn
The xv day thus shal be tyde
The world shal brenne on ilk a syde
and þe erthe there wee dwell
to the forþest end of hell
Thys tellis Ierome these tokens fifftene
as he in hebrews in book had hem seen.
The ferst day of the xv days
The see schal ryse as the bok says
abeuyn the heyeste of ony mounteyne
ffourtene cubites this is certeyne
and euene in his stede up stonde
as hyl doth here & euene londe
The secunde day it schal be lowe
That unnethes men schal it knowe
The thridde day it schal seme pleyn
And stondyn euene in his cors ageyn
As it stod at the begynnyng
Without yn rysyng or fallyng
The iiii day thus schal it be
The moste wunder fysches of the see
Schul come togedere & mak roryng
fful hedous to mannes understondyng
But what that they schal signefye
Wot no man but god onlye
The V day the see schal brennyn
and the wateres alle ther in
and that schal laste fro the sunne rysyng
Tyl the tyme of the doun goyng
The vi day schal springe a blody dew
On gres & tres as they schal schew
The vii day byggynges els schal doun falle
and grete casteles & toures with alle
The viii day roches & stones
Schal stryue togedere alle atones
and ech to other schal stryk faste
So that echon schal doun be caste
and ech ston on [?]ele weys
schal be sundryd on [?]ele partayes
The ix day schal erthedene be
General in euery cuntre
So gret erthedene as schal be than
Was neuer sythyn the word [sic] began
The x day after the nyne
The erthe schal be mad pleyn & euene
hilles & valeys schal turnyd be
and be mad euene to the see
The xi day men schaln comyn oute
of caues & hilles al aboute
and as wode men that no wit can
and non schaln speken to other than
The xii day after the sterres alle
and the signe fro heuene schal falle
The xiii day schal dede menes bones
Be set togeder & rysyn at ones
and upon on her graues stande
This schal be falle in ech a lande
The xiiii day alle that leuys than
Schuln deyen bothe man & wymman
ffor with hem schuln rysyn ageyn
That deydyn beforn to ioye or peyn
The xv day schal be tyde
The Word [sic] schal brennyn on euery syde
ffro the erthe ther we now dwelle
To the uttereste ende of helle
Thus tellyth Ierom of these toknes fiftene
as he in ebru boks hadde sene.
þe ferste day of þe fiftene dayes
þe see schal rise as þe book sayeþ [sic]
Aboue þe heigte of uche mountayne
fful fourety cubites certayne
And his steede euen up stande
As an hig hil doiþ on þe londe
þe secounde day þe see schal be lowe
þat uneþe men schul hym knowe
þe þridde day þe see schal seme playne
And stonde euen in his cours agayne
As it stod firste at þe begynnynge
Wiþ oute more risynge or fallynge
þe ferþe schal suche a wonder bee
þat þe moste wonderful fische of þe see
Schal come to giders & make suche a rourynge
þat it schal be hydous to mennes herynge
But þat rourynge schal signyfy
No man may wite but god almygty
þe fyfte day þe see schal brenne
And alle þe watres as þei schul renne
And þat schal laste from þe sonne risynge
To þe laste of þe sonne doun goynge
þe sixte day schal spinge [sic] a blody dew
On grees on trees as it schal schewe
þe seueþe day byggynges schul doun falle
And þe grete casteles & þe toures wiþ alle
þe eigteþ day þe harde roches & stones
Schul struke togideres al at ones
And uche of hem schal oþer doun caste
And uche agayne oþer hurtel faste
So þat uche ston on dyuerce [sic] wise
Schal breke oþer on þree partyes
þe nynþe day gret erden [sic] schal bee
Generaly in uche a contre
And so gret erþeden as schal be þan
Was neuere herde siþþen þe world be gan
X þe tenþe day þer after to nemen
hillus & valeis schul be turned euen
In to playne & maad faire to see
In al manere contre
þe eleueþ day men schul come oute
Of caues & of holes & wende al abougte
As woode men þat no witt can
And non schal speke to oþer þan
þe twelfe day after þe sterres alle
And þe signes from heuen schul falle
þe þrittenþe day deed men bones
Schul be sette togideres & rise al atones
And aboue on her graues stonde
þis schal be falle in uche londe
þe fourtenþe day alle þat lyueþ þanne
Schul dey[?]e childe man & womman
ffor þei schul wiþ hem arise agayne
þat bifore were dede or to ioyze or to payne
þe fiftenþe day þus schal betydde
þat þe world schal brenne on uche a syde
And þe erþe where we now dwelle
un to þe under ende of helle
þus telluþ Ierom þese tokenes fiftene
As he in Ebrewes bookes haþe sene
Þe furste day of Þo fytene days
Þe see schal ryse Þe book says
Aboue Þe hegest hul or mountayne
[60v] Fourty Kebyttys large serteynne
And in his stedde euun upstonde
As her [erasure] doth hul up on Þe londe [paraph heads this line, not next]
The secounde day hit schal be lowe
Þat unnethe hit schal be knowe
The Þridde day hit schal be pleyn
And stonde euun in his cours agayn
As hit stod at Þe bygynnyng
With outen rysyng or fallyng
Þe ferthe day Þus schal hit beo
Þe wondorfullest fysches of Þe se
Schul come to gedor and make Roryng
Wondur hedowos to monus knowyng
But what Þat hit schal seynefye
Wot non bot god al mygty
The fithe day Þe see schal brenne
And wattores alle Þat buth Þerinne
And lastes from Þe sonnne [sic] a rysyng
? euun to Þe tyme of here doun goyng
Þe syxte day schal sprynge blody dew
On erthe on gras fully to schew
Þe seeuenethe day buydyngus schun [sic] falle
And grete casteles and toures alle
Þe eygtethe day roches and stonns [sic]
Schun smyte to gederes alle at onns
Eycheon to oÞer schal smyte fast
So Þat eychon schal doun be cast
And geuuer eyche ston on synder wyse
Schun be partut in Þre partyse
Þe nenthe day schal erthe duyne beo
Ful generally in eyche contre
So gret Þondor as Þat schal be Þan
Was neuur seygen [erasure] Þe world bygan
Þe tenÞe day al forte nemne
Þe erthe schal be made al pleyn an geuene
Hulles and dalus schulne turned be
And be made geuun an playn to se
Þe elleuenthe day men schuln comen oute
Of cauus and holus ouuer al a bout
As wod men Þat no wyt konnen
[61v] And noon schal speke to oÞer Þenne
Þe tolfethe day sterres schun alle
And Þe sonne from heuene doun falle
Þe Þorttenthe day schuln mennes bonnes [schuln inserted by sigil place marker]
Be sett to gedour all at onnes
And up on her graue stonde
Þys schal byfalle in geuur eyche londe
Þe fourtenÞe day all Þat lyuun Þane
Schul dyge mon child and womanne
For Þey wyth hem schun ryse aygeyn
Þat dyeden by fore to ioy or peyn
Þe fyftenethe day schal Þus bytyde
Þe world schal brenne on euurey syde
And Þe erthe Þat we in dwelle
In to Þe utturmast part of helle
Ierom telleth Þese tokenesse fyftene
As he in ebrew bokes hade hem sene.
þe firste day of þe xv dayes
þe see shall [erasure] ryse þe boke sayes
aboue þe heygest cop of any mountayn
ffourty cubites þat is certayn
And euene yn hys styde up stonde
As an hull here doþ uppon þe londe
þe secounde day hit shal be so lowe
Þat men unneþe shullen hit knowe
þe þridde day hit shal seme pleyne
and stonde euene yn hys cours ageyne
as hit stood at þe bygynnyng
with oute rysyng or fallyng
þe ferþe day þus shal hit be
þat þe greete fysshes of þe se
Shul come to gider and make roryng t

But what hit shal signefye
Woot noon but god almygtye
þe fifte day þe see shal brynne
and alle þe waterys þat are þerynne
and þat shal laste fro þe sonne rysyng
Til here tyme of doun goyng
þe sixte day shal sprynge blody dew
On gras and trees deolful to shewe
þe seuenþe day bildyngges shul doun falle
and greete castellys and tourys alle
þe eygte day roches and stones
Shulle smyte to gider alle at ones
uche to oþir shal smygte fast
So þat uche shal be doun cast
and uche stoon yn dyuerse wyse
Shal be broken in þre partyse
þe nynþe day shal erþe quake be
Generally in uche countre
So gret dyn as shal be þan
Was neuer seþ þe wordle [sic] bygan
þe tenþe day forto neuene
al þe erþe shal be mad pleyn and euene

þe elleuenþe day men shul se out

þe twelþe day sterres alle
and the sonne from heuen shal falle

Be sett to gider alle at onys
and uppon her graues stonde
þis shal byfalle in uche londe

ffor þey wiþ hem shul ryse ageyn
þat di[?]ed byfore to ioye or peyn
þe fiftenþe day shal þis bityde
þe wordle shal brenne yn uche syde
and þe erþe þat we yn dwelle
unto þe utmest party of helle
Ierom telleþ þyse toknes fiftene
as he yn hebrew bokes had hem sene.
The furste of þese fyftene dayes
þe se schal a ryse as þe bok sayes
aboue þe erþe of eche monteyne
fful xl cubyts certeyne
and in her stede euene up stonde
as a hye hylle doþ in þe londe
The secunde day þe se echal be so lowe
þat oneþe eny man schal her y knowe
þe þrydde day þe se schal bycome playn
and stonde in hur cours euene agayn
as hit stod furst in þe bygynnyng
With oute more reysyng of [sic] gallyng
The ferþe day such a wonder schal be
þat þe moste wonderful fysches of þe se
Schul come to gedere and make roryng
þat hit schal be hydows to mann [erasure] heryng
Bote what schal þe roryng sygnyfye
Wot no man bote god almygthye
The fyfthe day þe se schal brenne
and alle þe waters as þei schul renne
and þat schal lest fro þe [sic] sonne a rysing
To þe tyme of sonnne down goyng
The syxte day schal sprynge a blody dewe
On gras and on tre as hit schal shewe
The seuende day byldyng schal doun falle
and grete castelys and towrs with alle
The eygtende day grete roches and stonys
Schul stryke to gedere alle a tonys
and ech of hem schal down oþer caste
and ech agens oþer schul hurtly faste
[sic]
So þat euery ston in dyuers wyse
Shul to smytten oþer in þre partyse
The neygende day gret erþequake schal be
Ouer al in euery lond and euery countre
ffor so grete erþe quaks as schul be þanne
Wes neuer hurd sutthe þe wordle by ganne
þe tenthe day after to nemne
Schal come a wynd down fro heuene
þat hullys and vallays turned schul be
In to playn and be mad euen to se
þe enleuede day men schul come out
of cauys and hullys and wende about
as wode men þat nougt can
and none schul spete [sic] to oþer than
þe twelfþe day aftur þe sterrs alle
and the syngnys fro heuene schul falle
þe þyrtenede day schul dede men bonys
Be set to gedere and a rise a tonys
and up on here grauys stylle stonde
þis schal falle by fore in euery londe
þe fortenede day al þat lyueþ þan
Schul deye child man and woman
ffor þei schul þanne with hem aryse agayn
þat afore wer ded to ioye or pay [sic]
þe fyftened day schal þus bytyde
þe wordle schal brenne in ech syde
al þe erþe þer we ynne dwelle
In to þe utter part of helle
þus telleþ Ierom þe toknys fyftene
as he in a bok of þe ebrewys haþ sene
[59r] The firste of þese fiftene days
Þe see shal rise as þe book says
Aboue þe heygte of þe mountayne
Ffully fourty cubitis certayne
And in his steede he euene up stonde [“he” crossed out]
As an hey hyl doth ouir þe londe
The secunde day þe see shal be so lowe
Þat anethis any man shal it knawe
The þridde day þe see shal seme pleyne
And stonde euene in his cours ageyne
As it stod first at þe beginnyng
With outin more rising or more fallyng
The feerthe shal swiche a wonndir be
Þe moste wonndirful fisshis of þe se
Shuln come togedere and make roryng
Þat shal ben hydous to mannis heryng
But what þat roryng shal singnify [sic]
No man may wite but god almygty
The fifte day þe see shal brenne
[59v] And alle þe wateris as þei shuln renne
And þat shal laste fro þe sonne rysing
Til þe tyme of þe sonne doun going
The sixte day shal springe a blody dewe
On gres on tre as it shal shewe
The seuenthe day biggingis shuln doun falle
And grete castellis and towris with alle
The eygthe day grete rochis and stonis
Shuln fygte togedere alle at onis
And eche on of hem shal oþir doun caste
And eche on agens othir shal hortele faste
So þat eche ston in diuers wyse
Shal sundre oþir in þre partyse
The nynthe day gret erdene [sic] shal be
Generali in eche cuntre
And so gret erdedene as þanne shal ben
Sythe þe werd [sic] began was neuere herd ne sen
The tenthe day þer aftir to neuene
Shal come a wynde fro heuene
Þat hillis and valeys turnyd shuln be
Into pleyn and mad euene to se
The elleuenthe day men shul come owte
Of cauis and holis and wende abowte
As wode men þat no wit kanne
And noon shal speke to oþir þanne
The twelfthe day aftir þe sterris alle
And þe singnis [sic] fro heuene shuln falle
The þrittenthe shuln deede mennis bonis
Be set togidere and ryse alle at onis
And up on here grauis stonde
Þis shal befalle in eche a londe
The fortenthe day al þat lyuith þanne
Shuln deyge child man and womanne
Ffor þei shuln with hem ryse ageyne
Þat before were deede to ioye or peyne
The fiftenthe day þus shal it be tyde
Þe werd [sic] shal brenne on eche a syde
And þe erde þer we now dwelle
In to þe uttrest ende of helle
Þus tellith Ierom þese tookins [sic] fiftene
[60r] As he in þe bookis of hebreuis hath sene.
Þe first day of þe fyften
Þe se sale rys als þe bok says
O bouyn þe heght of ilk mountayne
Ffourty cubyttes for soth sertayne
And euen in his stede upe stande
Als hilles her dos one þe lande
Þe seconde day hit sale be lawe
Þat unnethes men sale hit knawe
Þe thride day sale hit sem playne
And stand euyn in his cours o gayne
Als hit stode at þe begynnyng
With owtoun rysyng or fallyng
Þe ferth day þus sale hit be
Þe most wonderful ffysches in þe se
Sale come togedur & make romyng
Fful hydous unto mans heryng
What þat it sall sygnefy
Wot noman bot gode all myghty
Þe fyft day þe se sale bryn
And þe watyrs als þai ryn
And þat sale last fro þe sone rysyng
Unto þe tyme of þe doune gauggyng [sic]
Þe sext day sale spryng a blode dew
One gres & tres als þai sall [sic] schewe
Þe seuent day sale byggyngs doune fall
And gret castils & toures wyt all
Þe viii day roches and stones
Sale strike to geder all at ones
Ilkone tile eþer sale strike faste
So þat þai sale be all doune caste
And ilk stone one dyuers wys
Sale be sonderde [sic] in thre þartys
Þe ix day sale erth dyn be
Generally in ilk contre
[next leaf, recto] So gret erth dyn als sale be þan
Was neuer sen þe worlde began
Þe x day after to neuen
Þe erth sale be made playn & euyn
Hilles & valays sale turned be
And be made all heune to se
Þe xi day men sale come oute
Of caues & of hoyles aboute
Als wode men þat no wyt cane
And non sale speke tile oþer þane
Þe xii day after þe sterus all
And þe sygnes fro þe heuen sall fall
Þe xiii day sale ded mans bones
Be set to geder & rys at ones
And apoun þer graues stande
Þis sale be fall in ilk a lande
Þe xiiii day all þat lyues þan
Sale dy chylde man & woman
For þai wyt þam sall [sic] rys ogayn
Þat dide before tile ioy or þayn
Þe xv day sale þus be tyde
Þe worlde sale bryne one ilk a syde
And þe erth were we now duell
Unto þe uttirst ende of all hell
Þus tels Ierome þis tokyns xv
Als he in ebrew bokes has sen.
[166vb] Þo firste day of þo fiften dayes [paraþh in left margin]
Þo see sal rise as þo boke sais
Abouen þe heght of ilk mountayne
Ffourty cubittes of mett Certayne
& euen in his stede up stande
As hill here dos on þo lande
Þe secunde day it schall be lawe
Þat unnethes men schall it knawe
Þe thride day schall it seme playne
& stande euene in his cours agayne
As it stude at þo bygynnyng
Withouten risyng or fallynge
Þe fourte day þus schal it be
Þe moste wonderful fisches of þe see
Schal com togedir & make rounynge
Ffull hidus unto mannes herynge
Bot what it sal signifye
Wote no man bot gode onely
Þo fifte day þo se sal brenne
& wateres all þat are þerinne
& þat sal laste fro þe sunn risynge
Unto þo tyme of downe gangynge
Þe sexte day schall sprynge blody dewe
On gresse & trees as þat sall schewe
Þo seuent day biggynges sal doune fall
& grete casteles & toures withal
Þo eghte day roches & stones
Schall stryke togedyr al at ones
[167ra] Ilk one til oþer schal strik faste
So þat ilkon sal doune be caste
& ilk stone on sere wise
Schall be sundered in thre partyes
Þo nente day schall erth dyn be
Generally in ilk a cuntre
So grete erth dyn as schall be þan
Was neuer sene sen þo worlde began
Þo tente day after for to neuen
Þe erth sal be made playne & euen
Hilles & vailes sal turnede be
& be made all euen for to se
Þe eleuent day men sal com oute
Of caues of holes all aboute
As wode men þat no wit can
& none sal speke till oþer þan
Þo twelfte day after þo sternes all
& þo sunne fro heuen sall fall
Þo thritten day sall dedes mennes bones
Be sett togedyr & rise at ones
& up on her graues stande
Þis sall befall in ilk a lande
Þo fourten day all þat lifen þan
Schal dy both childe man & woman
Ffor þai with þame sal rise agayne
Þat dyede byfore to ioye & payne
Þo fiften day schall þus betyde
Þo worlde schal brenne on ilk a syde
& þo erth þer we now dwell
Unto þe uttereste pit of hell
Þus telles ierome þise tokenes fiftene
Als he in ebriw boke hade sene.
[142, a verso] Þe fyrst day of þa [sic] fyftene dayes
Þe see [?] sall ryse als þa bukes sayes
Abowue þe heght of ylka mowntayne
Ffully fowrty cubyttes certayne
And in hys stede euen uppe stande
Als a heghe hylle dos on þe lande
Þe secund day þe se sall be so lawe
Þat unnethes men may it knawe
Þe thryd day þe se sall seeme playne
And stand euen in hys cowrs agayne
Als it stode fyrst at þe begynnyng
With owten more rysyng or fallyng
Þe ffyerthe day sall swylk a wonndyr be
Þe moste wowndyrfull fysches of þe se
Sall com to gyder and mak swylk romying
[143, a recto] Þat it sall be hydowse to mannes heryng
Bot what þat romying sall sygnyfy
No man wayte [sic] bot godd alle myghty
Þe ffyft day þe see sall brynne
And alle þe wattyres þan sall rynne
And þat sall laste fro þe þe sonne rysyng
To þe tyme of þe sonn [sic] downe gangyng
Þe sext day sall spryng a blody dewe
On grysse and treese als it sall schewe
Þe seuent day byuggyng downe sall falle
And grete castelles and towres with alle
Þe aghtend day hard roches and stanes
Sall stryke togyder alle at anes
And ylkane of þanne [sic, see day 14] sall othyr down caste
And ylkane agayne othyr hortell faste
So þat ylk a stane on dyuers wyse
Sall sondyr othyr in seere [sic] þartyse
Þe neghen day grete erthe dynne sall be
Generaly in ylke cowntre
And so grete erthe dynne als sall be þan
Was neuer herd sen þe worlde began
Þe tyende day þar eftyr to neuen
Þe erthe sall be made playne and euen
Ffor hylles and vallayse sall turned be
In tyll playne and made euen to se
Þe elleuend day men salle com owte
Of caues and hooles and weende abowte
Als wode men þat no wytte kan
And none sall speke to othyr þan
Þe twelft day eftyr þe sternnes sall alle [“sall” crossed out]
And þe sygnes fro hefen sall falle
Þe thryttend day sall dede men banes
[144, a verso] Be sett togyder and ryse alle at anes
And abowne on þaire grafes stande
Þis sall befall in ylk a lande
Þe ffowrtend day alle þat lyffes þan
Sall dye chylde man and womman
Ffor þai solde ryse with þanne agaynne
Þat before war dede owthyr to ioye or payne
Þe ffyftende day þus sal betyde
Alle þe worlde sall brynne on ylk syde
And þe erthe whar we nowe dwelle
Untyl þe uttyrrest ende of alle helle
Þus telles Ierome þere [sic] tokenes fyftene
Als he in ys [sic] bukes of hebrewse had sene.
[recto, the inside of the second leaf of the bifolium] Þe fyft day Þo see schal brenne
And watres alle Þat are Þerynne
To laste fro Þe sunne rysyng
Tyl Þo tyme of her down goyng
Þe syxte day schal springe blodi dew
On gres and treus ful to schew
Þe seuente day byldynges schul down falle
And grete castelus & tourus alle
Þo eygte day rochus and stonus [“Ecce et videte” in later hand written in margin]
Schul smyte togeder alle atonus
Ilke to oÞer schul smyte so fast
Þat ilkon schal down be cast
And ilke stoon in dyuers wysus
Schal be sundred in Þre partyus
Þo nynte day schal erÞeden be [“e” superscripted above “r”]
Generally in ilke a cuntre
So gret erÞeden as schal be Þan [“e” superscripted above “r”]
Was neuer sythe Þo world began
Þo tenÞe day for to neuen
ErÞe schal be made pleyn & euen [no “e” superscript]
Hillus & valeyus schul turnyd be
And be made euene for to se
Þe eleuente day men schul cum out
Of cauus & of holys about
Os wode men Þat no wit can
For noon schal spekyn to oÞer Þan
Þo tweluete day Þo sterrus alle
And Þo sunne fro lyfne [sic] schal falle
Þo thryttente day alle Þat lyuen Þan
Schal dey chyld man and wymman
Ffor Þei wyth hem schul ryse ageyne
Þat yede before to ioye or peyn
Þe [sic] fyftente day schal Þus betyde
Þo world schal brenne on euery syde
And als Þo erÞe Þat we in dwelle [“e” superscripted above “r”]
Unto Þo uttermost Þarty of helle
[next leaf, a verso; outside of the second leaf of the bifolium. Lines faded, especially in middle]
Ierom techyth [?] Þo tokenus fyftene
Os he in ibru bokus hath sene.
